1	---
2	title: Examples
3	description: Copy-paste recipes — from a single curl to a production routing wrapper.
4	sidebar:
5	  order: 3
6	---
7	
8	import { Aside } from '@astrojs/starlight/components';
9	
10	Copy-paste recipes against the hosted API, from a single `curl` to a production routing wrapper. Every snippet is self-contained.
11	
12	<Aside type="tip" title="Setup">
13	Set your key once: `export MUBIT_API_KEY=[REDACTED] The Python examples use the [`minima_client`](/client-sdk/) package and read the same key. All requests go to `https://api.minima.sh`.
14	</Aside>
15	
16	## 1. Quickstart with curl
17	
18	Exercise the core endpoints with nothing but `curl` and `jq` — the fastest way to confirm your key works.
19	
20	```bash
21	# Service health (the only keyless endpoint)
22	curl -s https://api.minima.sh/v1/health | jq
23	
24	# A recommendation
25	REC=$(curl -s https://api.minima.sh/v1/recommend \
26	  -H "authorization: Bearer [REDACTED_BEARER_TOKEN]" \
27	  -H 'content-type: application/json' \
28	  -d '{"task":{"task":"Classify this support ticket by urgency.","task_type":"classification"},
29	       "cost_quality_tradeoff":2}')
30	echo "$REC" | jq '.recommended_model.model_id, .decision_basis, .recommendation_id'
31	
32	# Close the loop
33	curl -s https://api.minima.sh/v1/feedback \
34	  -H "authorization: Bearer [REDACTED_BEARER_TOKEN]" \
35	  -H 'content-type: application/json' \
36	  -d "{\"recommendation_id\":$(echo "$REC" | jq .recommendation_id),
37	       \"chosen_model_id\":\"claude-haiku-4-5\",\"outcome\":\"success\",\"quality_score\":0.92}" | jq
38	```
39	
40	## 2. The core loop
41	
42	The whole value loop with the Python SDK: recommend → (you run the model) → feedback. Report realized tokens and cost so the cost ranking sharpens.
43	
44	```python
45	from minima_client import MinimaClient
46	
47	with MinimaClient("https://api.minima.sh", api_key="mbt_…") as minima:
48	    rec = minima.recommend(
49	        "Summarize this incident report into 3 bullet points.",
50	        cost_quality_tradeoff=3,
51	        baseline_model_id="claude-opus-4-8",  # what you'd use without Minima -> honest savings
52	    )
53	    print(rec.recommended_model.model_id, rec.decision_basis)
54	
55	    # ... run rec.recommended_model.model_id in your own stack ...
56	
57	    minima.feedback(
58	        rec.recommendation_id,
59	        rec.recommended_model.model_id,
60	        "success",
61	        quality_score=0.95,
62	        input_tokens=1180,
63	        output_tokens=320,
64	        actual_cost_usd=0.0028,
65	        verified_in_production=True,
66	    )
67	```
68	
69	## 3. Constraints and the slider
70	
71	Hard `Constraints` (provider whitelist, quality floor, cost ceiling, deny-list) plus sweeping `cost_quality_tradeoff` from 0→10 to watch Minima walk the cost-vs-quality frontier for the same task.
72	
73	```python
74	from minima_client import MinimaClient, TaskInput, Constraints
75	
76	task = TaskInput(task="Refactor this 200-line module for readability.", task_type="code")
77	
78	with MinimaClient("https://api.minima.sh", api_key="mbt_…") as minima:
79	    rec = minima.recommend(
80	        task,
81	        constraints=Constraints(
82	            allowed_providers=["anthropic", "google"],
83	            min_quality=0.8,
84	            max_cost_per_call=0.02,
85	        ),
86	    )
87	    print("constrained pick:", rec.recommended_model.model_id)
88	
89	    for cq in (0, 5, 10):
90	        r = minima.recommend(task, cost_quality_tradeoff=cq)
91	        print(f"slider {cq:>2}: {r.recommended_model.model_id}  "
92	              f"~${r.recommended_model.est_cost_usd:.4f}")
93	```
94	
95	## 4. Multi-step workflow
96	
97	`POST /v1/recommend/workflow` routes each step of a pipeline independently — a cheap model for classify/extract, a stronger one for the hard reasoning step — and reports total cost versus the all-premium baseline. Each step gets its own `recommendation_id` for per-step feedback.
98	
99	```python
100	from minima_client import MinimaClient, WorkflowRequest, WorkflowStep, TaskInput
101	
102	req = WorkflowRequest(
103	    steps=[
104	        WorkflowStep(step_id="extract",
105	                     task=TaskInput(task="Extract entities from the email.",
106	                                    task_type="extraction")),
107	        WorkflowStep(step_id="reason",
108	                     task=TaskInput(task="Decide the next action given the entities.",
109	                                    task_type="reasoning", difficulty="hard")),
110	    ],
111	    cost_quality_tradeoff=4,
112	)
113	
114	with MinimaClient("https://api.minima.sh", api_key="mbt_…") as minima:
115	    wf = minima.recommend_workflow(req)
116	    for step in wf.steps:
117	        print(step.step_id, "→", step.recommendation.recommended_model.model_id)
118	    print(f"total ${wf.total_est_cost_usd:.4f} vs all-premium ${wf.total_est_cost_if_all_premium:.4f}")
119	```
120	
121	## 5. Zero-code intake
122	
123	`minima_client.autocapture` auto-captures your existing LLM calls into Minima's memory with no call-site changes — useful for backfilling history from traffic you already run. It needs a Mubit key for the underlying memory.
124	
125	```python
126	from minima_client import autocapture
127	
128	autocapture.enable(api_key="[REDACTED]", endpoint="https://api.mubit.ai",
129	                   namespace="team-payments", user_id="svc-router")
130	
131	# ... your normal OpenAI / Anthropic / LiteLLM / Gemini calls run here, auto-captured ...
132	
133	autocapture.feedback(good=True)   # learn does NOT fabricate a success signal — close it explicitly
134	autocapture.disable()
135	```
136	
137	## 6. Production routing wrapper
138	
139	The shape you'd ship: recommend a model, run it via the official **Anthropic SDK** (streaming, real token usage), then feed the realized cost/quality back.
140	
141	```python
142	import anthropic
143	from minima_client import AsyncMinimaClient
144	
145	async def routed_call(minima: AsyncMinimaClient, client: anthropic.AsyncAnthropic,
146	                      prompt: str, *, cost_quality_tradeoff: float = 4) -> str:
147	    rec = await minima.recommend(prompt, cost_quality_tradeoff=cost_quality_tradeoff)
148	    model = rec.recommended_model.model_id
149	
150	    async with client.messages.stream(
151	        model=model, max_tokens=1024,
152	        messages=[{"role": "user", "content": prompt}],
153	    ) as stream:
154	        msg = await stream.get_final_message()
155	
156	    text = "".join(b.text for b in msg.content if b.type == "text")
157	    cost = rec.recommended_model.est_cost_usd  # or compute from msg.usage + your price table
158	    await minima.feedback(
159	        rec.recommendation_id, model, "success",
160	        quality_score=0.95,
161	        input_tokens=msg.usage.input_tokens,
162	        output_tokens=msg.usage.output_tokens,
163	        actual_cost_usd=cost,
164	        verified_in_production=True,
165	    )
166	    return text
167	```
168	
169	## 7. Measure your savings
170	
171	The measurement loop: declare your default model on `recommend`, report realized cost on `feedback`, then read the ledger. Both baselines are reported side by side — `vs_premium` (generous) and `vs_declared` (your own default; the honest one).
172	
173	```python
174	from minima_client import MinimaClient
175	
176	with MinimaClient("https://api.minima.sh", api_key="mbt_…") as minima:
177	    rec = minima.recommend(
178	        "Classify this ticket by urgency.",
179	        baseline_model_id="claude-opus-4-8",   # the model you'd have used
180	    )
181	    # ... run rec.recommended_model.model_id in your stack ...
182	    minima.feedback(
183	        rec.recommendation_id, rec.recommended_model.model_id, "success",
184	        quality_score=0.95, actual_cost_usd=0.0009,    # realized, not estimated
185	        input_tokens=420, output_tokens=80,
186	    )
187	
188	    report = minima.savings(days=30, group_by="task_type")
189	    est = report.summary.estimated
190	    print(f"saved ${est.savings_vs_declared_usd:.4f} vs your default "
191	          f"over {est.n_declared} calls "
192	          f"(coverage {report.health['feedback_coverage']:.0%})")
193	
194	    cal = minima.calibration(days=30)
195	    print("global ECE:", cal.reports[0].ece)   # 0 = predictions match reality
196	```
197

1	// Generates plain-markdown twins of every .mdx page into public/, so the
2	// "Copy as Markdown" button can fetch them at runtime.
3	//
4	// JSX components are converted to readable markdown:
5	//   <PageHeader/>          -> stripped
6	//   <Tabs><Tab title=X>..  -> #### X heading per tab
7	//   <Steps><Step title=..  -> numbered list
8	//   <CardGroup><Card ..>   -> bullet list with title + href
9	//   <Note>/<Warning>/<Tip> -> blockquote
10	//   <Accordion title=X>    -> ### X + body
11	//   <Frame caption=X>      -> figure body + italic caption
12	//   <MubitTerminal/>       -> "live demo" note
13	//   any other JSX tag      -> tag stripped, inner content preserved
14	//
15	// Code (fenced + inline backticks) is protected with an alphanumeric token
16	// before transformation and restored at the end so contents aren't mangled.
17	
18	import fs from 'node:fs/promises'
19	import path from 'node:path'
20	import { fileURLToPath } from 'node:url'
21	
22	const HERE = path.dirname(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url))
23	const ROOT = path.resolve(HERE, '..')
24	const PAGES_DIR = path.join(ROOT, 'pages')
25	const OUT_DIR = path.join(ROOT, 'public')
26	
27	function parseAttrs(attrString) {
28	  const attrs = {}
29	  if (!attrString) return attrs
30	  const re =
31	    /(\w+)\s*=\s*(?:"([^"]*)"|'([^']*)'|\{(?:"([^"]*)"|'([^']*)'|([^}]+))\})/g
32	  let m
33	  while ((m = re.exec(attrString))) {
34	    attrs[m[1]] = m[2] ?? m[3] ?? m[4] ?? m[5] ?? (m[6] ?? '').trim()
35	  }
36	  return attrs
37	}
38	
39	function findClosingTag(src, tagName, openEnd) {
40	  const openRe = new RegExp('<' + tagName + '(?:\\s[^>]*)?>', 'g')
41	  const closeRe = new RegExp('</' + tagName + '\\s*>', 'g')
42	  let depth = 1
43	  let pos = openEnd
44	  while (pos < src.length) {
45	    openRe.lastIndex = pos
46	    closeRe.lastIndex = pos
47	    const o = openRe.exec(src)
48	    const c = closeRe.exec(src)
49	    if (!c) return null
50	    if (o && o.index < c.index) {
51	      depth++
52	      pos = o.index + o[0].length
53	    } else {
54	      depth--
55	      if (depth === 0) return { start: c.index, end: c.index + c[0].length }
56	      pos = c.index + c[0].length
57	    }
58	  }
59	  return null
60	}
61	
62	function replaceTag(src, tagName, openTransform, selfTransform) {
63	  if (selfTransform) {
64	    const selfRe = new RegExp('<' + tagName + '((?:\\s[^/>]*)?)\\s*/>', 'g')
65	    src = src.replace(selfRe, (_, attrStr) => selfTransform(parseAttrs(attrStr)))
66	  }
67	  const openRe = new RegExp('<' + tagName + '((?:\\s[^>]*)?)>', 'g')
68	  let result = ''
69	  let lastIdx = 0
70	  let m
71	  while ((m = openRe.exec(src))) {
72	    const openStart = m.index
73	    const openEnd = openStart + m[0].length
74	    const attrs = parseAttrs(m[1])
75	    const close = findClosingTag(src, tagName, openEnd)
76	    if (!close) break
77	    const inner = src.slice(openEnd, close.start)
78	    result += src.slice(lastIdx, openStart)
79	    result += openTransform(attrs, inner)
80	    lastIdx = close.end
81	    openRe.lastIndex = close.end
82	  }
83	  result += src.slice(lastIdx)
84	  return result
85	}
86	
87	function parseFrontmatter(src) {
88	  const m = src.match(/^---\n([\s\S]*?)\n---\n?/)
89	  if (!m) return { meta: {}, body: src }
90	  const meta = {}
91	  for (const line of m[1].split('\n')) {
92	    const idx = line.indexOf(':')
93	    if (idx === -1) continue
94	    const key = line.slice(0, idx).trim()
95	    let value = line.slice(idx + 1).trim()
96	    value = value.replace(/^['"]|['"]$/g, '')
97	    if (key) meta[key] = value
98	  }
99	  return { meta, body: src.slice(m[0].length) }
100	}
101	
102	function protectCode(src) {
103	  const blocks = []
104	  function tokenFor(match) {
105	    const i = blocks.length
106	    blocks.push(match)
107	    return 'XMDCODE' + i + 'ZZ'
108	  }
109	  let out = src
110	  out = out.replace(/```[\s\S]*?```/g, tokenFor)
111	  out = out.replace(/`[^`\n]+`/g, tokenFor)
112	  return { protectedSrc: out, blocks }
113	}
114	
115	function restoreCode(src, blocks) {
116	  return src.replace(/XMDCODE(\d+)ZZ/g, (_, i) => blocks[Number(i)])
117	}
118	
119	function transformBody(body) {
120	  // Protect code FIRST so import-stripping can't reach into code blocks.
121	  const { protectedSrc, blocks } = protectCode(body)
122	  let out = protectedSrc
123	
124	  // Strip ESM imports/exports (single-line only — must not span newlines).
125	  out = out.replace(/^[ \t]*import\s+[^\n]+?from\s+['"][^'"]+['"];?[ \t]*\n/gm, '')
126	  out = out.replace(/^[ \t]*import\s+['"][^'"]+['"];?[ \t]*\n/gm, '')
127	  out = out.replace(/^[ \t]*export\s+[^\n]+;?[ \t]*\n/gm, '')
128	
129	  out = replaceTag(out, 'PageHeader', () => '', () => '')
130	  const terminalNote =
131	    '\n_(Interactive terminal demo — view the live page for the animated walkthrough.)_\n'
132	  out = replaceTag(out, 'MubitTerminal', () => terminalNote, () => terminalNote)
133	
134	  out = replaceTag(out, 'Tabs', (_attrs, inner) => {
135	    const collected = []
136	    replaceTag(inner, 'Tab', (attrs, tabInner) => {
137	      const label = attrs.title || attrs.label || 'Tab'
138	      collected.push('\n#### ' + label + '\n\n' + tabInner.trim() + '\n')
139	      return ''
140	    })
141	    return '\n' + collected.join('\n') + '\n'
142	  })
143	
144	  out = replaceTag(out, 'Steps', (_attrs, inner) => {
145	    const items = []
146	    let i = 0
147	    replaceTag(inner, 'Step', (attrs, stepInner) => {
148	      i++
149	      const head = attrs.title ? '**' + attrs.title + '**\n\n   ' : ''
150	      const lines = stepInner.trim().split('\n').join('\n   ')
151	      items.push(i + '. ' + head + lines)
152	      return ''
153	    })
154	    return '\n' + items.join('\n\n') + '\n'
155	  })
156	
157	  function renderCard(attrs, inner) {
158	    const desc = inner.trim().replace(/\s+/g, ' ')
159	    const title = attrs.title || ''
160	    if (attrs.href) {
161	      return '- **[' + title + '](' + attrs.href + ')** — ' + desc
162	    }
163	    return '- **' + title + '** — ' + desc
164	  }
165	
166	  out = replaceTag(out, 'CardGroup', (_attrs, inner) => {
167	    const cards = []
168	    replaceTag(inner, 'Card', (attrs, cardInner) => {
169	      cards.push(renderCard(attrs, cardInner))
170	      return ''
171	    })
172	    return '\n' + cards.join('\n') + '\n'
173	  })
174	
175	  // Catch any stray <Card> outside a <CardGroup>.
176	  out = replaceTag(out, 'Card', renderCard)
177	
178	  for (const tag of ['Note', 'Warning', 'Tip']) {
179	    out = replaceTag(out, tag, (_attrs, inner) => {
180	      const body = '**' + tag + ':** ' + inner.trim()
181	      const quoted = body.split('\n').map((l) => '> ' + l).join('\n')
182	      return '\n' + quoted + '\n'
183	    })
184	  }
185	
186	  out = replaceTag(out, 'Accordion', (attrs, inner) => {
187	    return '\n### ' + (attrs.title || 'Details') + '\n\n' + inner.trim() + '\n'
188	  })
189	
190	  out = replaceTag(out, 'Frame', (attrs, inner) => {
191	    let body = inner.trim()
192	    if (attrs.caption) body += '\n\n_' + attrs.caption + '_'
193	    return body
194	  })
195	
196	  // Strip remaining JSX wrappers. Iterate until stable for nested wrappers.
197	  let prev
198	  do {
199	    prev = out
200	    out = out.replace(/<[A-Za-z][\w.-]*(?:\s[^>]*)?\/>/g, '')
201	    out = out.replace(/<[A-Za-z][\w.-]*(?:\s[^>]*)?>/g, '')
202	    out = out.replace(/<\/[A-Za-z][\w.-]*\s*>/g, '')
203	  } while (out !== prev)
204	
205	  out = restoreCode(out, blocks)
206	
207	  out = out.replace(/\n{3,}/g, '\n\n')
208	
209	  return out.trim() + '\n'
210	}
211	
212	async function walk(dir) {
213	  const entries = await fs.readdir(dir, { withFileTypes: true })
214	  const files = []
215	  for (const e of entries) {
216	    if (e.name.startsWith('.')) continue
217	    const full = path.join(dir, e.name)
218	    if (e.isDirectory()) files.push(...(await walk(full)))
219	    else if (e.name.endsWith('.mdx') || e.name.endsWith('.md')) files.push(full)
220	  }
221	  return files
222	}
223	
224	async function main() {
225	  let pages
226	  try {
227	    pages = await walk(PAGES_DIR)
228	  } catch (err) {
229	    if (err.code === 'ENOENT') {
230	      console.warn('[generate-md] no pages directory at ' + PAGES_DIR + ', skipping')
231	      return
232	    }
233	    throw err
234	  }
235	
236	  await fs.mkdir(OUT_DIR, { recursive: true })
237	
238	  let written = 0
239	  for (const file of pages) {
240	    const rel = path.relative(PAGES_DIR, file)
241	    const src = await fs.readFile(file, 'utf8')
242	    const { meta, body } = parseFrontmatter(src)
243	    const md = transformBody(body)
244	
245	    const header = []
246	    if (meta.title) header.push('# ' + meta.title)
247	    if (meta.description) header.push('\n> ' + meta.description + '\n')
248	    header.push('<!-- Auto-generated from pages/' + rel + ' — do not edit by hand. -->')
249	
250	    // Strip a duplicate H1 if the body opens with the same title as frontmatter.
251	    let bodyOut = md
252	    if (meta.title) {
253	      const dupRe = new RegExp('^#\\s+' + meta.title.replace(/[.*+?^${}()|[\]\\]/g, '\\$&') + '\\s*\\n', 'm')
254	      bodyOut = bodyOut.replace(dupRe, '')
255	    }
256	
257	    const final = header.join('\n') + '\n\n' + bodyOut
258	    const outRel = rel.replace(/\.(md|mdx)$/, '.md')
259	    const outPath = path.join(OUT_DIR, outRel)
260	    await fs.mkdir(path.dirname(outPath), { recursive: true })
261	    await fs.writeFile(outPath, final, 'utf8')
262	    written++
263	  }
264	  console.log('[generate-md] wrote ' + written + ' .md file(s) into ' + path.relative(ROOT, OUT_DIR) + '/')
265	}
266	
267	main().catch((err) => {
268	  console.error('[generate-md] failed:', err)
269	  process.exit(1)
270	})

1	---
2	title: Concepts
3	description: How Mubit's memory model works, what the learning loop does, and how it compares to vector DBs, prompt caches, and RAG.
4	---
5	
6	import { CardGroup, Card, Note, Warning, Tip, Steps, Step, Frame, Accordion, Tabs, Tab, MubitTerminal, PageHeader } from '@components'
7	
8	<PageHeader />
9	
10	# Concepts
11	
12	How Mubit's memory model works, what the learning loop does, and how it compares to vector DBs, prompt caches, and RAG.
13	
14	Mubit's job is to give AI agents memory that persists, evolves, and is queryable across runs. This page is an evaluator's overview — read it to decide whether Mubit fits your problem before you write any integration code.
15	
16	## The memory model
17	
18	Mubit organizes memory by **entry type**. Each type has different retention, retrieval, and access semantics.
19	
20	| Entry type | What it stores | Lifetime | Recall scope |
21	| --- | --- | --- | --- |
22	| **fact** | Atomic statements grounded in observation (e.g. "Customer Taylor uses SSO") | Run-scoped by default | Same `session_id` + `agent_id` |
23	| **lesson** | Generalized takeaways from runs (e.g. "Always flag bare `except:`") | Configurable: `run` / `session` / `global` | Cross-session if `lesson_scope="global"` |
24	| **rule** | Hard constraints that must always apply (e.g. "Never PII-log a `user.ssn`") | Persistent | Cross-session |
25	| **trace** | Per-call records of what happened (LLM call, tool call, response) | Run-scoped | Same `session_id` |
26	| **archive_block** | Exact artifacts you'll need byte-for-byte later (diffs, SQL, raw outputs) | Persistent | Cross-session via `reference_id` |
27	
28	Storing a fact and asking the wrong session to recall it returns nothing. Storing a global lesson and asking any session keyed by the same `user_id` returns it. This is the most common shape of "why didn't my recall work" — see [SDK helpers → cross-session recall](/sdk/sdk-helpers#cross-session-recall).
29	
30	### More entry types for richer agents
31	
32	The five types above are what most agents start with. The full taxonomy is larger — multi-agent coordination, tool-call audit, and process-reward learning each have their own entry types.
33	
34	| Entry type | What it captures |
35	| --- | --- |
36	| **observation** | Raw agent observations during a run |
37	| **tool_input** / **tool_output** | LLM tool call request and response |
38	| **reflection** | Notes a reflection pass produced before lessons crystallize |
39	| **task_result** | Outcome of a discrete sub-task |
40	| **log** | Free-form structured log entries |
41	| **checkpoint** | Snapshot of context for later restore — see [SDK methods → helper catalog](/sdk/sdk-methods#current-helper-catalog) |
42	| **step_outcome** | Per-step success/failure for process-reward learning — see [Step-level outcomes](/recipes/step-level-outcomes) |
43	| **mental_model** | Curated high-priority summary, retrieved first |
44	| **handoff** | Inter-agent task handoff record — see [Lane-scoped memory](/recipes/lane-scoped-memory) |
45	| **feedback** | Verdict on a handoff |
46	
47	## The learning loop
48	
49	The four-step loop is what differentiates Mubit from a passive store. Read [How Mubit works](/sdk/how-mubit-works) for the full discussion.
50	
51	<Steps>
52	  <Step title="Write">
53	    Your agent calls `remember()` to store facts, traces, or observations as they happen.
54	  </Step>
55	  <Step title="Retrieve">
56	    Before the next LLM call, `recall()` finds relevant evidence and `get_context()` assembles a token-budgeted context block.
57	  </Step>
58	  <Step title="Reflect">
59	    After a run completes, `reflect()` extracts reusable lessons from what happened. Fresh lessons start as candidates and are validated before they're trusted — a lesson stays pending until enough evidence accrues, then is promoted (run → session → global) or marked stale. See [How Mubit works](/sdk/how-mubit-works) for details.
60	  </Step>
61	  <Step title="Reinforce">
62	    `record_outcome()` feeds success / failure signals back into the lesson store, strengthening what worked and weakening what didn't.
63	  </Step>
64	</Steps>
65	
66	The agent gets better over multiple runs without retraining the model.
67	
68	## How Mubit compares
69	
70	| If you need… | Reach for… | Mubit's role |
71	| --- | --- | --- |
72	| Similarity search over a static corpus | A vector DB (Pinecone, Weaviate, pgvector) | Not the right tool — Mubit is for **mutable** memory with outcomes |
73	| Prompt-level caching of identical requests | A prompt cache (Anthropic prompt cache, Helicone) | Orthogonal — Mubit can sit alongside |
74	| Stateless RAG against a fixed knowledge base | A RAG framework (LlamaIndex, LangChain) | Use the framework + Mubit as its persistent store via the [adapters](/sdk/framework-integrations) |
75	| Memory that persists across sessions and evolves | **Mubit** | The whole product |
76	| Multi-agent coordination with scoped access | **Mubit** (`register_agent`, `handoff`, `feedback`) | Native primitives |
77	
78	## Trust and operations
79	
80	- **Authentication.** All calls use a bearer API key formatted as `mbt_<instance>_<key_id>_<secret>`. The instance segment routes to a region; the key id is safe to log; the secret is not. See [Authentication](/sdk/authentication).
81	- **Scopes.** Each agent registers with explicit `read_scopes` and `write_scopes` (`fact`, `lesson`, `rule`, `archive_block`, etc). The runtime enforces them.
82	- **Rate limits.** Per-instance, per-route. See [Rate limits](/sdk/rate-limits).
83	- **Audit.** Every write produces an activity entry. See [Activity & audit trail](/sdk/activity-audit).
84	- **Data residency / SOC 2.** See [mubit.ai/security](https://mubit.ai/security).
85	
86	## Where to go next
87

90	    The full memory model, control-plane vs. core-plane, query and context controls, and a worked helper-first example.
91	  </Card>

94	  </Card>

96	    Plug Mubit into CrewAI, LangGraph, LangChain, LlamaIndex, ADK, Agno, Vercel AI SDK, or MCP.
97	  </Card>
98	  <Card title="Pricing" icon="receipt" href="https://mubit.ai/pricing">

100	  </Card>

## Comprehensive Documentation Site Analysis

### 1. **Current Stack**

**Framework:** Astro with Starlight theme
- **Astro version:** 5.7.5

- **Config files:**
  - `/Users/eldaru/Mubit/Minima/minima/docs-site/astro.config.mjs`
  - `/Users/eldaru/Mubit/Minima/minima/docs-site/tsconfig.json`

### 2. **Content Structure**

**Location:** `/Users/eldaru/Mubit/Minima/minima/docs-site/src/content/docs/`

**File inventory (6 pages, 1,037 total lines):**
- `index.mdx` — Homepage/splash page with hero and feature cards
- `getting-started.mdx` — Getting Started guide (authentication, prerequisites, core loop)
- `concepts.mdx` — Detailed conceptual docs (algorithm, cost tiers, learning loop, degradation)
- `api-reference.mdx` — Complete API endpoint reference with all schemas
- `client-sdk.mdx` — Python SDK documentation (MinimaClient, AsyncMinimaClient, autocapture)
- `examples.mdx` — Copy-paste examples (curl, Python, production routing, measurement)

**Content organization:** Flat structure using Starlight's loader; no nested subcategories. All `.mdx` files with embedded `import` statements for Starlight components (Steps, Aside, Tabs, Card, Badge, etc.).

### 3. **Content Inventory & Topics**

| Page | Topics Covered |
|------|---|
| **Getting Started** | Mubit API key prerequisites, base URL, 3-step recommendation loop (recommend → run → feedback), cold start behavior, next steps |
| **Concepts** | Problem statement, recommend-only architecture, the feedback loop, non-parametric k-NN algorithm, task classification, candidate selection, recall + aggregation, cost-quality slider (0–10), cost-basis tiers (estimate/observed/rescaled), LLM escalation logic, feedback learning loop, degradation/fallback behavior, how predictions improve over time (cold start → warming → mature), measurement endpoints |
| **API Reference** | Auth (pass-through Mubit key), error handling (RFC 7807), `POST /v1/recommend`, `POST /v1/recommend/workflow`, `POST /v1/feedback`, `GET /v1/models`, `GET /v1/strategies`, `GET /v1/savings`, `GET /v1/calibration`, `GET /v1/health`, plus full request/response schemas, warnings, and error codes |
| **Client SDK** | `MinimaClient` & `AsyncMinimaClient` (sync/async), `recommend()`, `recommend_workflow()`, `feedback()`, `models()`, `strategies()`, `health()`, `savings()`, `calibration()`, error handling (`MinimaError`), zero-code intake (`autocapture`), monkeypatching for OpenAI/Anthropic/LiteLLM/Google-GenAI |
| **Examples** | 7 progressive examples: 1) curl quickstart, 2) core Python loop with baseline, 3) constraints + slider sweep, 4) multi-step workflow, 5) zero-code intake (autocapture), 6) production routing wrapper (Anthropic SDK + streaming + feedback), 7) savings measurement (baseline + realized cost) |
| **Home** | Hero with tagline, 4-card feature overview (Recommend, Run yourself, Feed back, Improve automatically), 6 key properties (recommend-only, memory-driven, cost ranking, cold start, provable, bring your own memory) |

### 4. **Config & Branding**

**Starlight configuration** (`astro.config.mjs`):
- **Title:** "Minima"
- **Description:** "A recommendation engine for LLM model routing. Cuts token spend without losing quality."
- **Logo:** `./src/assets/logo.svg` (word-mark "minima" with orange-red "m")

**Sidebar navigation structure:**
```
Start Here

Reference
├── API Reference

Guides
└── Examples
```

**Brand colors** (from `custom.css`):

- **Dark mode:** Brighter orange `#f0703a` on deep warm charcoal `#161614`
- **Accent low:** `#fde8e2` (light) / `#3a1206` (dark)

**Typography:** Bold sans-serif, tight letter-spacing (-0.02em), h1 at 800 weight; active sidebar entry styled as solid accent pill.

### 5. **Build & Deploy**

**Build process:**

  - `npm run dev` → `astro dev`
  - `npm run build` → `astro build`
  - `npm run preview` → `astro preview`

- **Nginx config:** `/Users/eldaru/Mubit/Minima/minima/docs-site/nginx.conf`
  - Listens on port 8080
  - Gzip compression enabled for text/js/json/svg/fonts
  - Security headers: X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options, Referrer-Policy
  - Aggressive caching for `/_astro/*` (1 year, immutable)
  - 30-day cache for images/fonts/static assets

### 6. **Assets**

**No other static assets** in `public/` or `src/assets/`.

---

### **Key Takeaways for Migration to Vocs:**

1. **Content is well-structured**: 6 documentation pages totaling 1,037 lines, all `.mdx` format with embedded Starlight components. The content uses:
   - `Card`, `CardGrid` for feature showcase
   - `Steps` for procedural guides

3. **Navigation is flat**: 3 top-level sections (Start Here, Reference, Guides) with 5 content pages total. No nested subcategories.

4. **Starlight components will need replacement**: `Card`, `CardGrid`, `Steps`, `Aside`, `Tabs`, `TabItem`, `Badge` from Starlight will need to be replaced with Vocs equivalents or custom MDX components.

5. **Build infrastructure is production-ready**: Docker multi-stage build + nginx with proper caching, gzip, security headers. Netlify fallback also configured.

## 4. Custom Components & Theming

**Custom React Components (exported from components/index.ts):**

```typescript
export { Card } from './Card'
export { CardGroup } from './CardGroup'
export { Note, Warning, Tip } from './Note'
export { Steps, Step } from './Steps'
export { Frame } from './Frame'
export { Accordion } from './Accordion'
export { Tabs, Tab } from './Tabs'
export { MubitTerminal } from './Terminal/MubitTerminal'
export { PageHeader } from './PageHeader'
export { CopyMarkdownButton } from './CopyMarkdownButton'
export { ChatBox } from './ChatBox'
export { DocsChatPanel } from './DocsChatPanel'
```

**Component Details:**

- **Card & CardGroup**: Grid layout system with icon support. Card accepts `title`, `icon` (FontAwesome name), `href`, and children. Icons are inline SVG path definitions (30+ icons hardcoded).

- **Note, Warning, Tip**: Styled callouts with emojis and color-coded left borders. Theme colors:
  - Note: blue (`rgba(96, 165, 250, ...)`)
  - Warning: yellow/amber (`rgba(251, 191, 36, ...)`)
  - Tip: green (`rgba(74, 222, 128, ...)`)

- **Steps & Step**: Numbered step lists with optional titles. Renders as `<ol>` with styled items.

- **Tabs & Tab**: Multi-language tabs (Python, Node.js, Rust, curl). Maintains state across renders. Used for code samples.

- **Accordion**: Collapsible sections with title. Opens/closes state managed internally.

- **Frame**: Image/code frame container with optional caption underneath (italicized).

- **MubitTerminal**: **Complex interactive component** — renders an xterm.js terminal window with live script execution. Supports language switching (Python/Node/Rust). Uses lazy loading + Suspense. Hardcoded scripts imported from `Terminal/script.*.js`.

- **PageHeader**: Shows the current page section title (e.g., "Overview", "Build with Mubit") using `usePageData` hook aliased to deep Vocs internals and `getSectionForPath` helper from sidebar.ts.

- **CopyMarkdownButton & DocsChatPanel & ChatBox**: Custom utilities (markdown export, chat integrations).

**Theming & Branding:**

- **Primary accent color:** `#ff4500` (orange-red) — used for:
  - Links and highlighted text
  - Card hover states
  - CTA button background (`/styles/global.css`)
  - Icon fills in cards
  
- **Logo variants:** Both light + dark SVGs required:
  - `public/images/logo-light.svg`
  - `public/images/logo-dark.svg`

- **Custom CSS (public/styles/global.css):**
  - Only ~60 lines
  - Styles the "Get API Key" top-nav link as a prominent filled button (not a plain link)
  - Uses Vocs' CSS variables: `--vocs-color_backgroundAccent`, `--vocs-color_backgroundAccentText`, etc.
  - Includes hover effects (translateY, box-shadow)
  - Respects `prefers-reduced-motion`

- **Theme dark/light variables:** Vocs provides default Vocs-branded light/dark themes. Customizations are done via CSS variables and the `theme.accentColor` config.

**Vocs Patches (patch-package):**

File: `/Users/eldaru/Mubit/Documentation/docs/patches/vocs+1.4.1.patch`

Customizations:
1. **Footer:** Removes the "Edit this page on GitHub" link (left only "Last updated" date, no time)
2. **CopyMarkdownButton:** Outline injection for markdown export feature

**Frontmatter Fields:**

- Use `<Note>`, `<Warning>`, `<Tip>` for callouts
- Use `<Steps>` + `<Step title="X">` for numbered procedures

## 6. Build & Deployment

**Build Scripts (package.json):**

```json
{
  "scripts": {
    "generate-md": "node scripts/generate-md.mjs",
    "predev": "node scripts/generate-md.mjs",
    "dev": "vocs dev",
    "prebuild": "node scripts/generate-md.mjs",
    "build": "vocs build",
    "preview": "vocs preview --host 0.0.0.0 --port ${PORT:-8080}",
    "typecheck": "tsc --noEmit",
    "postinstall": "patch-package"
  }
}
```

**Build Flow:**
1. **`npm run dev`** → runs `generate-md.mjs` (pre-hook) → starts Vocs dev server on localhost:5173
2. **`npm run build`** → runs `generate-md.mjs` (pre-hook) → runs `vocs build` → outputs to `dist/`
3. **`npm run preview`** → serves `dist/` on `:8080` (matches production)
4. **`npm run typecheck`** → validates TypeScript

**Docker Build (Dockerfile):**

```dockerfile
# Stage 1: Build
FROM node:22-alpine
RUN apk add --no-cache git
COPY package.json package-lock.json .npmrc ./
COPY patches ./patches
RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/root/.npm npm ci --no-audit --no-fund
RUN npx patch-package  # Apply vocs patches explicitly

ARG VITE_PUBLIC_POSTHOG_KEY=""
ARG VITE_PUBLIC_POSTHOG_HOST="https://eu.i.posthog.com"
ENV VITE_PUBLIC_POSTHOG_KEY=$VITE_PUBLIC_POSTHOG_KEY
ENV VITE_PUBLIC_POSTHOG_HOST=$VITE_PUBLIC_POSTHOG_HOST

RUN node scripts/generate-md.mjs
RUN npm run build

# Stage 2: Runtime (nginx)
FROM nginx:1.27-alpine
ENV PORT=8080
RUN rm /etc/nginx/conf.d/default.conf
COPY nginx.conf.template /etc/nginx/templates/default.conf.template
COPY --from=builder /app/dist /usr/share/nginx/html
EXPOSE 8080
```

**Nginx Config (nginx.conf.template):**

```nginx
server {
    listen ${PORT};
    root /usr/share/nginx/html;
    index index.html;

    # Long-cache versioned static assets (1 year)
    location ~* \.(?:js|css|woff2?|svg|png|jpg|jpeg|gif|ico|webp|wasm)$ {
        expires 1y;
        add_header Cache-Control "public, immutable";
    }

    # Proxy /chat to internal backend service
    location = /chat {
        proxy_pass ${CHAT_BACKEND_URL}/chat;
        proxy_buffering off;  # Required for SSE
        proxy_read_timeout 300s;
    }

    # SPA fallback: deep links → index.html
    location / {
        try_files $uri $uri.html $uri/ /index.html;
    }

    # Health check
    location = /healthz {
        return 200 "ok\n";
    }
}
```

**Environment Variables (.env / .env.local):**

```bash
PORT=8080  # Cloud Run / nginx port
VITE_PUBLIC_POSTHOG_KEY=<your-key>
VITE_PUBLIC_POSTHOG_HOST=https://eu.i.posthog.com
```

**Deployment Target:** Cloud Run (Google Cloud) — hence the `$PORT` env var handling and `/healthz` endpoint.

---

## Key Customization Touchpoints for Your Replication

To adapt this for another product, you'll need to update:

1. **vocs.config.ts:**
   - `title`, `description`
   - `logoUrl` (light + dark SVGs)
   - `iconUrl` (favicon)
   - `theme.accentColor` (brand color)
   - `topNav` links and labels
   - `sidebar` structure (via components/sidebar.ts)

2. **Branding assets:**
   - `public/images/logo-light.svg`
   - `public/images/logo-dark.svg`
   - `public/images/favicon.png`

3. **Colors in custom components:**
   - Callout box colors in `Note.tsx`
   - Card icon color in `Card.tsx`
   - Global CTA button colors in `public/styles/global.css`

4. **Content (pages/):**
   - Rewrite all `.mdx` files
   - Keep folder structure and routing convention

5. **Sidebar config (components/sidebar.ts):**
   - Redefine section groups and links

6. **Docker/nginx:**
   - Update `CHAT_BACKEND_URL` if you have backend services
   - Update PostHog key or remove analytics

All the heavy lifting (framework, theming system, component library) is ready to reuse—just swap the content, branding, and navigation.</result>

    ``` **Frontmatter Fields:** - `title` — Page heading + browser title - `description` — Meta description + sidebar hint - `showOutline` — Hide/show right-side TOC (optional) - `content.width`, `content.horizontalPadding` — Override default layout (optional) **MDX Conventions:** - All components imported at top with `import { ... } from '@components'` - Components always come from `@components` alias - Always include `` near the top (shows section breadcrumb) - Use `` + `` for multi-language code examples - Use `` + `` for visual call-to-action sections - Use ``, ``, `` for callouts - Use `` + `` for numbered procedures - Inline CSS via `style={{...}}` for layout overrides (Vocs supports this) - Markdown code blocks use triple-backticks with optional `title` attribute: ` ```python title="script.py" ` **Concepts Page (pages/concepts.mdx):** Uses standard Markdown tables, `` for the learning loop, and `` for comparisons. Shows rich use of semantic HTML + components. --- ## 6. Build & Deployment **Build Scripts (package.json):** ```json { "scripts": { "generate-md": "node scripts/generate-md.mjs", "predev": "node scripts/generate-md.mjs", "dev": "vocs dev", "prebuild": "node scripts/generate-md.mjs", "build": "vocs build", "preview": "vocs preview --host 0.0.0.0 --port ${PORT:-8080}", "typecheck": "tsc --noEmit", "postinstall": "patch-package" } } ``` **Build Flow:** 1. **`npm run dev`** → runs `generate-md.mjs` (pre-hook) → starts Vocs dev server on localhost:5173 2. **`npm run build`** → runs `generate-md.mjs` (pre-hook) → runs `vocs build` → outputs to `dist/` 3. **`npm run preview`** → serves `dist/` on `:8080` (matches production) 4. **`npm run typecheck`** → validates TypeScript **Docker Build (Dockerfile):** ```dockerfile # Stage 1: Build FROM node:22-alpine RUN apk add --no-cache git COPY package.json package-lock.json .npmrc ./ COPY patches ./patches RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/root/.npm npm ci --no-audit --no-fund RUN npx patch-package # Apply vocs patches explicitly ARG VITE_PUBLIC_POSTHOG_KEY="" ARG VITE_PUBLIC_POSTHOG_HOST="https://eu.i.posthog.com" ENV VITE_PUBLIC_POSTHOG_KEY=$VITE_PUBLIC_POSTHOG_KEY ENV VITE_PUBLIC_POSTHOG_HOST=$VITE_PUBLIC_POSTHOG_HOST RUN node scripts/generate-md.mjs RUN npm run build # Stage 2: Runtime (nginx) FROM nginx:1.27-alpine ENV PORT=8080 RUN rm /etc/nginx/conf.d/default.conf COPY nginx.conf.template /etc/nginx/templates/default.conf.template COPY --from=builder /app/dist /usr/share/nginx/html EXPOSE 8080 ``` **Nginx Config (nginx.conf.template):** ```nginx server { listen ${PORT}; root /usr/share/nginx/html; index index.html; # Long-cache versioned static assets (1 year) location ~* \.(?:js|css|woff2?|svg|png|jpg|jpeg|gif|ico|webp|wasm)$ { expires 1y; add_header Cache-Control "public, immutable"; } # Proxy /chat to internal backend service location = /chat { proxy_pass ${CHAT_BACKEND_URL}/chat; proxy_buffering off; # Required for SSE proxy_read_timeout 300s; } # SPA fallback: deep links → index.html location / { try_files $uri $uri.html $uri/ /index.html; } # Health check location = /healthz { return 200 "ok\n"; } } ``` **Environment Variables (.env / .env.local):** ```bash PORT=8080 # Cloud Run / nginx port VITE_PUBLIC_POSTHOG_KEY= VITE_PUBLIC_POSTHOG_HOST=https://eu.i.posthog.com ``` **Deployment Target:** Cloud Run (Google Cloud) — hence the `$PORT` env var handling and `/healthz` endpoint. --- ## Key Customization Touchpoints for Your Replication To adapt this for another product, you'll need to update: 1. **vocs.config.ts:** - `title`, `description` - `logoUrl` (light + dark SVGs) - `iconUrl` (favicon) - `theme.accentColor` (brand color) - `topNav` links and labels - `sidebar` structure (via components/sidebar.ts) 2. **Branding assets:** - `public/images/logo-light.svg` - `public/images/logo-dark.svg` - `public/images/favicon.png` 3. **Colors in custom components:** - Callout box colors in `Note.tsx` - Card icon color in `Card.tsx` - Global CTA button colors in `public/styles/global.css` 4. **Content (pages/):** - Rewrite all `.mdx` files - Keep folder structure and routing convention 5. **Sidebar config (components/sidebar.ts):** - Redefine section groups and links 6. **Docker/nginx:** - Update `CHAT_BACKEND_URL` if you have backend services - Update PostHog key or remove analytics All the heavy lifting (framework, theming system, component library) is ready to reuse—just swap the content, branding, and navigation.

<result>Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive view. Let me compile my findings into a detailed report:

## Comprehensive Investigation Report: Minima Harness CLI

Based on my thorough exploration of `/Users/eldaru/Mubit/Minima/minima/packages/tui/`, here are my complete findings for documentation planning:

---

### **1. WHAT IT IS**

**Product/Binary Name:** `minima`

**Package:** `minima-tui` (v0.1.0)

**What it does:** A TypeScript/Ink terminal UI and cost-aware model-routing coding agent that:
- Acts as a terminal-based AI coding assistant
- Routes prompts through the **Minima recommender service** (Python FastAPI backend) to intelligently select the best LLM model for each task
- Executes tool calls (read, write, edit, bash) with permission gating
- Maintains per-repo memory isolation via Mubit's memory backend
- Supports multiple LLM providers (OpenAI, Anthropic Claude, Google Gemini, DeepSeek, xAI, Groq, OpenRouter)
- Streams responses in real-time with costs & quality tracking

**Entry point / Command name:** `minima` (compiled binary from `/src/cli/main.ts`)

**Architecture highlight:** A faithful TypeScript port of the Python `minima_harness`, it talks to the Python FastAPI recommender service over HTTP (`/v1/*` endpoints) but **never proxies LLM calls itself** — those go directly to provider APIs.

**Key files:**
- `/Users/eldaru/Mubit/Minima/minima/packages/tui/README.md` — overview
- `/Users/eldaru/Mubit/Minima/minima/packages/tui/package.json` — binary entry point (`src/cli/main.ts`)

---

### **2. COMMANDS & FEATURES**

**Slash Commands** (interactive TUI mode):

From the `COMMANDS` array in `app.tsx` (line 73–97), the user-facing commands are:

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `/model` | Select or pin a model (or 'auto' to unpin) |
| `/clear` | Clear chat messages |
| `/auth` | Sign in to Mubit & provision this repo's project |
| `/config` | Show/set API keys (MUBIT, GEMINI, ANTHROPIC, etc.) |
| `/config set <KEY> <value>` | Store a credential |
| `/config get <KEY>` | Retrieve a credential |
| `/help` | Show available commands list |
| `/quit` / `/exit` | Exit the application |
| `/cost` | Show cost meter totals |
| `/reconnect` | Reconnect routing client |
| `/new` | Start a fresh session |
| `/name <label>` | Set the session display name |
| `/session` | Show session info |
| `/tree` | View the session tree |
| `/fork <entry-id>` | Fork from an entry ID |
| `/clone` | Clone the current session |
| `/resume [id]` | Resume a session (optionally by id) |
| `/judge [on\|off]` | Toggle LLM judging on/off |
| `/thoughts [on\|off]` | Toggle streaming model's thinking |
| `/mouse [on\|off]` | Toggle mouse capture (scroll vs select/copy) |
| `/perms` | Show current tool permission grants |
| `/undo` | Undo last AI change (git checkout) |
| `/compact` | Summarize old turns to free context |
| `/plan` | Toggle plan mode (read-only) |

**CLI Flags** (non-interactive modes):

From `main.ts` (lines 151–241):

```
minima [prompt] [--print|--mode json] [options]
minima auth              # sign in to Mubit + provision this repo's project
minima config [set|get]  # manage stored credentials

Options:
  -p, --print              # one-shot: print the reply and exit
  --mode {interactive|print|json}
  --model ID               # pin a model (bypasses routing)
  --provider NAME          # provider for a pinned --model
  --thinking LEVEL         # off|minimal|low|medium|high|xhigh
  --offline                # bypass Minima routing
  -t, --tools LIST         # comma-separated tool allowlist
  -xt, --exclude-tools LIST
  -nt, --no-tools
  -h, --help
```

**Run modes:**
- **Interactive TUI (default):** `minima` — full Ink UI with conversation, status bar, model picker
- **One-shot print:** `minima -p "explain this repo"` — run & print final answer, exit
- **Event stream:** `minima --mode json "refactor foo" | jq .` — stream AgentEvents as JSON lines for scripting

**Built-in Tools** (agents can invoke these with permission):

From `/src/tools/builtin.ts` (line 35–49):

1. **`read`** — Read file contents
2. **`write`** — Create new files
3. **`edit`** — Modify existing files
4. **`bash`** — Run shell commands (with timeout & abort support)
5. **`ls`** — List directory contents
6. **`glob`** — Glob pattern matching
7. **`grep`** — Text search with regex
8. **`todowrite`** — Special tool for plan/todo files
9. **`web_fetch`** — Fetch & parse HTTP content

Users can exclude tools via CLI: `-xt bash,write` or pin specific ones via `-t read,ls`.

**Terminal UI Features:**

From `app.tsx`:
- **Conversation view** with scrolling (Page Up/Down, Home/End, Ctrl+G/Ctrl+E)
- **Status bar** showing: model selection (pinned vs. Minima routing), cost totals, context %, token counts
- **Model picker** (Ctrl+L) — interactive selector for 8 default models + custom models
- **Command palette** (Ctrl+P) — slash-command autocomplete
- **Config overlay** (Ctrl+P → /config) — interactive editor for API keys
- **Permission overlay** — interactive grant/deny for sensitive tools (read-only auto-approves by directory)
- **Session picker** (/resume) — browse and resume previous session trees
- **Streaming display** — shows assistant reply + tool calls in real-time
- **Thinking display** (when enabled) — shows model's reasoning for models with extended thinking

**Keyboard Shortcuts** (from `app.tsx` lines 673–724):

| Key | Action |
|-----|--------|
| Ctrl+C (once) | Prepare to quit (Ctrl+C again to exit) |
| Ctrl+C (while running) | Abort the in-flight run |
| Esc | Abort the in-flight run |
| Ctrl+L | Open model picker |
| Ctrl+P | Open command palette |
| Ctrl+R | Toggle route mode (auto vs. confirm) |
| Ctrl+G | Jump to top of chat |
| Ctrl+E | Jump to bottom of chat |
| Page Up / Page Down | Scroll up/down |
| Shift+Space | Scroll down (alternate) |
| ↑/↓ in command/session picker | Navigate |
| ⏎ (Return) | Execute command/pick session |
| Tab | Auto-complete slash command |
| ↑/↓ at prompt | Cycle through input history |

**Permission System:**

From `permissions.ts` and `app.tsx`:
- **Read tools** (`read`, `ls`, `glob`, `grep`) — auto-approved within `cwd` and allowed parent directories; can grant "always" for a directory
- **Write tools** (`write`, `edit`) — always prompt; can grant "always" for the tool
- **Dangerous tools** (`bash`) — always prompt; can grant "always" for the tool
- **Plan mode** (`/plan`) — read-only toggle that blocks `write`, `edit`, `bash` completely

---

### **3. CONFIGURATION**

**Config file locations & format:**

From `config_store.ts`:

1. **Per-user secrets** (keychain-first):
   - **Primary:** OS keychain via `keytar` native module (macOS Keychain, Linux Secret Service, Windows Credential Manager)
   - **Fallback:** `~/.minima-harness/config.env` (0600 mode, plaintext)
   - **Format:** `KEY=VALUE` lines

2. **Project .env file** (project-scoped):
   - `.env.harness` or `.env` in the current directory
   - **Precedence:** Project .env > shell env > user store

3. **Per-repo project metadata:**
   - `~/.minima-harness/projects.json` — maps repo identity → Mubit instance/project/namespace

**Environment Variables:**

From `config.ts` and `config_store.ts`:

| Variable | Purpose | Example | Required? |
|----------|---------|---------|-----------|
| `MUBIT_API_KEY` | Routing auth & memory backend access | (secret key) | **Yes** |
| `MINIMA_URL` | Minima recommender endpoint | `https://api.minima.sh` (default) | No |
| `MINIMA_API_KEY` | Minima auth (optional; falls back to MUBIT_API_KEY) | (secret key) | No |
| `MUBIT_ENDPOINT` | Mubit memory backend URL | (overrides default) | No |
| `MUBIT_CONSOLE_URL` | Mubit console URL (used by `/auth`) | `https://console.mubit.ai` (default) | No |
| `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` | Claude API key | (secret key) | Conditional* |
| `OPENAI_API_KEY` | OpenAI API key | (secret key) | Conditional* |
| `GEMINI_API_KEY` | Google Gemini API key | (secret key) | Conditional* |
| `DEEPSEEK_API_KEY` | DeepSeek API key | (secret key) | Conditional* |
| `XAI_API_KEY` | xAI/Grok API key | (secret key) | Conditional* |
| `GROQ_API_KEY` | Groq API key | (secret key) | Conditional* |
| `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` | OpenRouter aggregator API key | (secret key) | Conditional* |
| `MINIMA_NAMESPACE` | Per-repo memory lane (project isolation) | (custom string) | No |
| `MINIMA_TIMEOUT` | Request timeout in seconds | `30` (default) | No |

*At least one provider key is needed to run the LLM.

**Supported Providers** (from `provider_catalog.ts`):

- **Anthropic** (Claude) — `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`, `ANTHROPIC_OAUTH_TOKEN`
- **OpenAI** — `OPENAI_API_KEY`
- **Google Gemini** — `GEMINI_API_KEY`, `GOOGLE_API_KEY`, `GOOGLE_GENAI_API_KEY`
- **DeepSeek** — `DEEPSEEK_API_KEY` (cheap, open-weight)
- **xAI (Grok)** — `XAI_API_KEY`
- **Groq** — `GROQ_API_KEY` (fast inference)
- **OpenRouter** — `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` (aggregator)

**Default Model Catalog** (from `main.ts` lines 64–143):

8 seed models (users can add more via `/model` picker):
- `gpt-4o-mini`, `gpt-4o` (OpenAI)
- `claude-haiku-4-5`, `claude-sonnet-4-6`, `claude-opus-4-8` (Anthropic)
- `gemini-2.5-flash`, `gemini-2.5-pro` (Google)
- `deepseek-chat` (DeepSeek)

**Config Sections** (from `config_store.ts`):

1. **LLM provider keys** — all provider API keys
2. **Mubit / Minima routing** — `MUBIT_API_KEY`, `MINIMA_URL`, auth keys

---

### **4. INSTALLATION / SETUP**

**Building from source:**

```bash
cd packages/tui
bun install
bun run check     # type-check
bun run lint      # biome check
bun test          # 75 hermetic tests
bun run build     # produces dist/minima (single native binary)
./dist/minima --help
```

**Prerequisites:**
- **Bun >= 1.2** (from `package.json` engines)
- **One provider API key** (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, DeepSeek, Groq, xAI, or OpenRouter)
- **MUBIT_API_KEY** (Mubit routing + memory backend access)

**First-time setup:**

1. **Set credentials:**
   ```bash
   minima config set MUBIT_API_KEY <your-mubit-key>
   minima config set OPENAI_API_KEY <your-openai-key>
   # or ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, GEMINI_API_KEY, etc.
   ```

2. **Or use the browser login flow (recommended):**
   ```bash
   minima auth
   # Opens browser → signs in to Mubit → provisions this repo's project → stores MUBIT_API_KEY
   ```

3. **Start the TUI:**
   ```bash
   minima
   ```

**Auth flow** (`/auth` command or `minima auth` CLI):
- Launches browser to Mubit console
- User authorizes
- Returns `MUBIT_API_KEY` + instance/project metadata
- Stores key securely (keychain or `~/.minima-harness/config.env`)
- Persists project mapping in `~/.minima-harness/projects.json` for per-repo isolation

**Config CLI** (`minima config`):
- `minima config` — list all config keys with masked secrets
- `minima config set <KEY> <value>` — store a credential
- `minima config get <KEY>` — retrieve a credential

---

### **5. ARCHITECTURE OVERVIEW (Brief)**

**High-level source structure:**

```
src/
├── cli/main.ts                  # CLI entry point: arg parsing, modes (interactive/print/json)
├── run_modes.ts                 # Non-interactive: --print, --mode json
├── index.ts                     # Package barrel export
├── types.d.ts                   # Global type definitions
│
├── ai/                          # LLM layer
│   ├── types.ts                 # Message, Usage, Model, AssistantMessage, etc.
│   ├── events.ts                # Streaming events: TextDeltaEvent, ThinkingEvent, etc.
│   ├── registry.ts              # Model catalog + registration
│   ├── provider_catalog.ts      # Provider metadata (env vars, API ids, blurbs)

│   │   ├── google.ts            # @google/genai
│   │   ├── openai-compat.ts     # raw fetch SSE (covers OpenAI, DeepSeek, xAI, Groq, OpenRouter)

├── tools/                       # Built-in tools

│
├── tui/                         # Terminal UI (Ink/React)
│   ├── app.tsx                  # HarnessApp: main component, keybindings, commands
│   ├── auth.ts                  # runAuth(): browser login flow

```

**Key integration flows:**

1. **Start (interactive):** `main() → loadEnvFiles() → seedDefaultModels() → new MinimaAgent() → render(HarnessApp) → event loop`

2. **Prompt → Response:**
   - User types → `onSubmit()` → `agent.promptRouted(text)` 
   - → Route via Minima (`/v1/route`)
   - → Run agent loop (stream LLM, execute tools, retry)
   - → Judge quality
   - → POST feedback to `/v1/feedback`

3. **Permission gating:** `beforeToolCall` hook checks permissions, renders overlay if needed, resolves promise.

4. **Session persistence:** Every turn appended to `~/.minima-harness/sessions/<sessionId>.jsonl`

5. **Built-in Tools**

All three explorations are complete. Here's the picture:

- **Reference (`../../Documentation/docs/`)** = **Vocs 1.4.1** (React/Vite, npm, `.mdx`), custom components (`Card`, `Note`, `Steps`, `Tabs`, `MubitTerminal`…), theming via `vocs.config.ts` + `components/sidebar.ts`, `patch-package` patches, Docker+nginx deploy.
- **Current Minima `docs-site/`** = **Astro + Starlight**, with 6 SDK pages already written (index, getting-started, concepts, api-reference, client-sdk, examples) and its own orange-red brand (`#e84524`).
- **Harness CLI (`packages/tui/`)** = the `minima` binary (Bun/Ink TUI): slash commands, CLI flags, built-in tools, config/auth, sessions, permissions, providers — no user docs yet, only a README.

So the core of this task is: rebuild `docs-site` on Vocs (matching the reference), port the existing SDK content from Starlight to Vocs, and author a brand-new Harness CLI section. A few decisions materially change the plan, so let me confirm them before I finalize.

# Minima Docs: Migrate to Vocs + Add Harness CLI Section

## Context

Minima (a Mubit product) needs a documentation site that matches the stack, design, and framework of the existing Mubit docs at `../../Documentation/docs/` — which is built on **Vocs 1.4.1** (React/Vite, `.mdx`, custom component library, `patch-package`, Docker+nginx deploy).

Today, Minima's `docs-site/` is built on **Astro + Starlight** and contains 6 SDK pages (index, getting-started, concepts, api-reference, client-sdk, examples). The goal:

1. **Rebuild `docs-site/` on Vocs**, reusing the reference's framework scaffolding, components, and theming.
2. **Port the existing SDK content** from Starlight `.mdx` into Vocs `.mdx` (swap component imports).
3. **Author a brand-new "Harness CLI" section** documenting the `minima` terminal agent in `packages/tui/`, which currently has no user docs beyond its README.

Decisions confirmed with the user:
- Replace Astro in-place (one site, no parallel `docs-vocs/`).
- Match the Mubit reference theming exactly: accent `#ff4500`, `JetBrains Mono`, dark color scheme.
- Two products separated by **top-nav tabs** (SDK · Harness CLI · API Reference), each with its own path-based sidebar.
- Harness install docs describe **build-from-source only** (accurate to the repo today).

---

## Approach

### Step 1 — Vendor the Vocs scaffolding from the reference

Copy the reusable infrastructure from `../../Documentation/docs/` into `docs-site/` (adapting, not blindly copying):

- **`components/`** — copy the presentational components and their `index.ts` barrel:
  - `Card`, `CardGroup`, `Note`/`Warning`/`Tip`, `Steps`/`Step`, `Tabs`/`Tab`, `Frame`, `Accordion`, `PageHeader`, `CopyMarkdownButton`.
  - Add a small **`Badge`** component (Starlight had one; Vocs does not) — a styled inline span — since the SDK API-reference page uses `<Badge>` for HTTP verbs.
  - **Skip** the backend-dependent components: `MubitTerminal` (hardcoded scripts + xterm), `ChatBox`, `DocsChatPanel` (need a chat backend). These are not needed for launch.
  - `components/sidebar.ts` — rewrite for Minima's nav (see Step 4), keeping the reference's path-keyed structure and `getSectionForPath` helper used by `PageHeader`.
- **`patches/vocs+1.4.1.patch`** — copy verbatim (removes the GitHub "edit this page" footer link; wires the copy-markdown outline). Applied via `postinstall`.
- **`scripts/generate-md.mjs`** — copy (generates `.md` twins for the "Copy as Markdown" button; runs in `predev`/`prebuild`).
- **`public/styles/global.css`** — copy (styles the "Get API Key" top-nav item as a filled CTA button).

### Step 2 — Rewrite build config for Vocs; delete Astro

- **`docs-site/package.json`** — replace Astro deps/scripts with the reference's Vocs set. **Pin `vocs@^1.4.1` and `react@^19` exactly** so `patches/vocs+1.4.1.patch` applies cleanly. Scripts: `generate-md`, `predev`, `dev` (`vocs dev`), `prebuild`, `build` (`vocs build`), `preview` (`vocs preview --host 0.0.0.0 --port ${PORT:-8080}`), `typecheck`, `postinstall` (`patch-package`).
- **`docs-site/vocs.config.ts`** — new file modeled on the reference:
  - `title: 'Minima'`, `titleTemplate: '%s · Minima'`, description from the existing Starlight config.
  - `theme: { accentColor: '#ff4500', colorScheme: 'dark' }`, `font: { google: 'JetBrains Mono' }`, `content.width: '1200px'`.
  - `logoUrl: { light, dark }` + `iconUrl` (favicon) — see Step 3.
  - `topNav`: `SDK` → `/sdk/getting-started` (match `/sdk`); `Harness CLI` → `/harness/overview` (match `/harness`); `API Reference` → `/api-reference/endpoints` (match `/api-reference`); `Get API Key` → `https://console.mubit.ai` (external CTA).
  - `sidebar` imported from `components/sidebar.ts`.
  - `head()` — inject `/styles/global.css`; keep the optional PostHog block gated on `VITE_PUBLIC_POSTHOG_KEY` (harmless if unset).
  - `vite.resolve.alias` for `@components` and the `usePageData` deep-import alias `PageHeader` needs.
- **`docs-site/tsconfig.json`** — swap Astro's config for the reference's React/Vocs tsconfig.
- **Delete Astro**: `astro.config.mjs`, `src/` (Astro content/assets/styles) — but only *after* content is ported (Step 5).

### Step 3 — Branding assets

- Produce light/dark logo SVGs at `public/images/logo-light.svg` and `public/images/logo-dark.svg` from Minima's existing `src/assets/logo.svg` (its "m" color is already dynamic; fix explicit fills per variant).
- Add `public/images/favicon.png` (or reuse the existing `public/favicon.svg` and point `iconUrl` at it — Vocs accepts SVG).
- Keep the wordmark as **Minima** (this is the Minima product's docs); theming/layout matches the reference.

### Step 4 — Navigation & sidebar structure

`components/sidebar.ts`, path-keyed:

- **`/sdk`**: *Start Here* → Getting Started, Concepts · *Reference* → Python Client SDK · *Guides* → Examples
- **`/harness`**: Overview · Installation · Configuration · Interactive TUI · CLI Usage · Tools & Permissions · Model Routing · Sessions · Troubleshooting
- **`/api-reference`**: API Reference (endpoints)

### Step 5 — Port existing SDK content (Starlight → Vocs)

Move each existing page from `src/content/docs/*.mdx` to `pages/` and swap the component layer:

| From (Starlight) | To (Vocs) |
| --- | --- |
| `index.mdx` | `pages/index.mdx` (home — "what is Minima" + two ways to use: SDK / Harness CLI cards) |
| `getting-started.mdx` | `pages/sdk/getting-started.mdx` |
| `concepts.mdx` | `pages/sdk/concepts.mdx` |
| `client-sdk.mdx` | `pages/sdk/client-sdk.mdx` |
| `examples.mdx` | `pages/sdk/examples.mdx` |
| `api-reference.mdx` | `pages/api-reference/endpoints.mdx` |

Component/import migration (mechanical, applied to every page):
- Replace Starlight import lines with `import { ... } from '@components'`; add `<PageHeader />` after frontmatter (reference convention).
- `CardGrid`→`CardGroup`; `Aside` (`note`/`tip`/`caution`/`danger`)→`Note`/`Tip`/`Warning`; `Steps`→`Steps`+`Step`; `Tabs`/`TabItem`→`Tabs`/`Tab`; `Badge`→ new `Badge`; `<Code>`→ fenced code blocks with ` ```lang title="..." `.
- Keep prose, tables, and code content unchanged — only the wrapping components change.

### Step 6 — Author the Harness CLI section (`pages/harness/*.mdx`)

New content derived from `packages/tui/` (`src/cli/main.ts`, `src/tui/app.tsx`, `src/tui/config_store.ts`, `README.md`):

- **`overview.mdx`** — what the `minima` harness is: a cost-aware, memory-isolated terminal coding agent that routes each prompt through the Minima recommender, runs the chosen model itself (never proxies), executes tools with permission gating, and feeds outcomes back.
- **`installation.mdx`** — prerequisites (Bun ≥ 1.2, `MUBIT_API_KEY`, ≥1 provider key); `bun install` → `bun run build` → `./dist/minima`; first-run `minima auth` (browser login + per-repo project provisioning) vs. manual `minima config set`.
- **`configuration.mdx`** — env vars (`MUBIT_API_KEY`, `MINIMA_URL`, `MINIMA_API_KEY`, provider keys, `MINIMA_NAMESPACE`, `MINIMA_TIMEOUT`); config precedence (project `.env`/`.env.harness` > shell env > user store); credential storage (keychain via `keytar`, fallback `~/.minima-harness/config.env`); supported providers + default model catalog.
- **`interactive.mdx`** — starting/quitting the TUI; status bar; keyboard shortcuts (Ctrl+L picker, Ctrl+P palette, Ctrl+R route mode, scroll keys, Ctrl+C abort/quit); slash commands (`/model`, `/clear`, `/help`, `/cost`, `/thoughts`, `/judge`, `/mouse`, `/name`, `/session`, …).
- **`cli.mdx`** — non-interactive modes: one-shot `minima -p`, JSON event stream `minima --mode json`, flags (`--model`, `--provider`, `--thinking`, `--offline`, `-t/-xt/-nt`), and the `minima auth` / `minima config [set|get]` subcommands.
- **`tools.mdx`** — built-in tools (`read`, `write`, `edit`, `bash`, `ls`, `glob`, `grep`, `web_fetch`, `todowrite`); the permission model (reads auto-approved by directory; writes/bash prompt; grant-always); `/perms`, `/undo`, and `/plan` read-only mode.
- **`routing.mdx`** — how routing works: route → run → judge → feedback loop, cost/quality slider intuition, pinning a model vs. `auto`, `--offline` fallback. Cross-link to the SDK Concepts page.
- **`sessions.mdx`** — append-only session tree in `~/.minima-harness/sessions/`; `/new`, `/resume`, `/fork`, `/clone`, `/tree`, `/compact`.
- **`troubleshooting.mdx`** — `/reconnect`, auth failures (`minima auth`), permission denials, context overflow (`/compact`), missing provider keys.

### Step 7 — Deploy config

- **`Dockerfile`** — adapt to the reference's two-stage pattern: `node:22-alpine` builder (`npm ci` → `npx patch-package` → `node scripts/generate-md.mjs` → `vocs build`) → `nginx:1.27-alpine` serving `dist/` with a `$PORT`-templated conf and SPA fallback (`try_files $uri $uri.html $uri/ /index.html`) + `/healthz`.
- **`nginx.conf`** — update for the Vocs `dist/` layout and SPA fallback (drop the `/_astro/*` rule; long-cache static assets; no-cache HTML).
- **`netlify.toml`** — unchanged in spirit: build `npm run build`, publish `dist/`, SPA redirect `/* → /index.html 200`.

---

## Critical files

**Create / heavily adapt (from reference):**
- `docs-site/vocs.config.ts`, `docs-site/tsconfig.json`, `docs-site/package.json`
- `docs-site/components/` (all listed components + `index.ts` + `sidebar.ts` + new `Badge`)
- `docs-site/patches/vocs+1.4.1.patch`, `docs-site/scripts/generate-md.mjs`
- `docs-site/public/styles/global.css`, `docs-site/public/images/{logo-light.svg,logo-dark.svg,favicon.png}`

**Content (create):**
- `docs-site/pages/index.mdx`
- `docs-site/pages/sdk/{getting-started,concepts,client-sdk,examples}.mdx` (ported)
- `docs-site/pages/api-reference/endpoints.mdx` (ported)
- `docs-site/pages/harness/{overview,installation,configuration,interactive,cli,tools,routing,sessions,troubleshooting}.mdx` (new)

**Adapt (deploy):** `docs-site/Dockerfile`, `docs-site/nginx.conf`, `docs-site/netlify.toml`

**Delete (after porting):** `docs-site/astro.config.mjs`, `docs-site/src/`

**Reference sources for harness content (read-only):**
- `packages/tui/src/cli/main.ts` (flags, subcommands, model catalog)
- `packages/tui/src/tui/app.tsx` (slash commands, keybindings, UI)
- `packages/tui/src/tui/config_store.ts` (config schema, storage)
- `packages/tui/README.md`

---

## Verification

1. `cd docs-site && npm install` — confirm `postinstall` applies `patches/vocs+1.4.1.patch` with no error (this is why versions must be pinned to `1.4.1`).
2. `npm run dev` → open `http://localhost:5173`:
   - Verify all three top-nav tabs (SDK · Harness CLI · API Reference) load and each shows its own sidebar.
   - Verify dark theme, `#ff4500` accent, JetBrains Mono, logo (light/dark), favicon.
   - Spot-check ported SDK pages render (Cards, Notes, Steps, Tabs, code blocks) with no missing-component errors.
   - Click through every new `harness/*` page.
3. `npm run typecheck` — clean.
4. `npm run build` — `dist/` builds with no errors/broken links; `npm run preview` on `:8080` serves it.
5. (Optional) `docker build docs-site/` then run and hit `/healthz` + a deep link to confirm SPA fallback.

- **`cli.mdx`** — non-interactive modes: one-shot `minima -p`, JSON event stream `minima --mode json`, flags (`--model`, `--provider`, `--thinking`, `--offline`, `-t/-xt/-nt`), and the `minima auth` / `minima config [set|get]` subcommands.
- **`tools.mdx`** — built-in tools (`read`, `write`, `edit`, `bash`, `ls`, `glob`, `grep`, `web_fetch`, `todowrite`); the permission model (reads auto-approved by directory; writes/bash prompt; grant-always); `/perms`, `/undo`, and `/plan` read-only mode.
- **`routing.mdx`** — how routing works: route → run → judge → feedback loop, cost/quality slider intuition, pinning a model vs. `auto`, `--offline` fallback. Cross-link to the SDK Concepts page.
- **`sessions.mdx`** — append-only session tree in `~/.minima-harness/sessions/`; `/new`, `/resume`, `/fork`, `/clone`, `/tree`, `/compact`.
- **`troubleshooting.mdx`** — `/reconnect`, auth failures (`minima auth`), permission denials, context overflow (`/compact`), missing provider keys.

### Step 7 — Deploy config

- **`Dockerfile`** — adapt to the reference's two-stage pattern: `node:22-alpine` builder (`npm ci` → `npx patch-package` → `node scripts/generate-md.mjs` → `vocs build`) → `nginx:1.27-alpine` serving `dist/` with a `$PORT`-templated conf and SPA fallback (`try_files $uri $uri.html $uri/ /index.html`) + `/healthz`.

- **`netlify.toml`** — unchanged in spirit: build `npm run build`, publish `dist/`, SPA redirect `/* → /index.html 200`.

## Approved Plan (edited by user):
# Minima Docs: Migrate to Vocs + Add Harness CLI Section

## Context

Minima (a Mubit product) needs a documentation site that matches the stack, design, and framework of the existing Mubit docs at `../../Documentation/docs/` — which is built on **Vocs 1.4.1** (React/Vite, `.mdx`, custom component library, `patch-package`, Docker+nginx deploy).

Today, Minima's `docs-site/` is built on **Astro + Starlight** and contains 6 SDK pages (index, getting-started, concepts, api-reference, client-sdk, examples). The goal:

1. **Rebuild `docs-site/` on Vocs**, reusing the reference's framework scaffolding, components, and theming.
2. **Port the existing SDK content** from Starlight `.mdx` into Vocs `.mdx` (swap component imports).
3. **Author a brand-new "Harness CLI" section** documenting the `minima` terminal agent in `packages/tui/`, which currently has no user docs beyond its README.

Decisions confirmed with the user:
- Replace Astro in-place (one site, no parallel `docs-vocs/`).
- Match the Mubit reference theming exactly: accent `#ff4500`, `JetBrains Mono`, dark color scheme.
- Two products separated by **top-nav tabs** (SDK · Harness CLI · API Reference), each with its own path-based sidebar.
- Harness install docs describe **build-from-source only** (accurate to the repo today).

---

## Approach

### Step 1 — Vendor the Vocs scaffolding from the reference

Copy the reusable infrastructure from `../../Documentation/docs/` into `docs-site/` (adapting, not blindly copying):

- **`components/`** — copy the presentational components and their `index.ts` barrel:
  - `Card`, `CardGroup`, `Note`/`Warning`/`Tip`, `Steps`/`Step`, `Tabs`/`Tab`, `Frame`, `Accordion`, `PageHeader`, `CopyMarkdownButton`.
  - Add a small **`Badge`** component (Starlight had one; Vocs does not) — a styled inline span — since the SDK API-reference page uses `<Badge>` for HTTP verbs.
  - **Skip** the backend-dependent components: `MubitTerminal` (hardcoded scripts + xterm), `ChatBox`, `DocsChatPanel` (need a chat backend). These are not needed for launch.
  - `components/sidebar.ts` — rewrite for Minima's nav (see Step 4), keeping the reference's path-keyed structure and `getSectionForPath` helper used by `PageHeader`.
- **`patches/vocs+1.4.1.patch`** — copy verbatim (removes the GitHub "edit this page" footer link; wires the copy-markdown outline). Applied via `postinstall`.
- **`scripts/generate-md.mjs`** — copy (generates `.md` twins for the "Copy as Markdown" button; runs in `predev`/`prebuild`).
- **`public/styles/global.css`** — copy (styles the "Get API Key" top-nav item as a filled CTA button).

### Step 2 — Rewrite build config for Vocs; delete Astro

- **`docs-site/package.json`** — replace Astro deps/scripts with the reference's Vocs set. **Pin `vocs@^1.4.1` and `react@^19` exactly** so `patches/vocs+1.4.1.patch` applies cleanly. Scripts: `generate-md`, `predev`, `dev` (`vocs dev`), `prebuild`, `build` (`vocs build`), `preview` (`vocs preview --host 0.0.0.0 --port ${PORT:-8080}`), `typecheck`, `postinstall` (`patch-package`).
- **`docs-site/vocs.config.ts`** — new file modeled on the reference:
  - `title: 'Minima'`, `titleTemplate: '%s · Minima'`, description from the existing Starlight config.
  - `theme: { accentColor: '#ff4500', colorScheme: 'dark' }`, `font: { google: 'JetBrains Mono' }`, `content.width: '1200px'`.
  - `logoUrl: { light, dark }` + `iconUrl` (favicon) — see Step 3.
  - `topNav`: `SDK` → `/sdk/getting-started` (match `/sdk`); `Harness CLI` → `/harness/overview` (match `/harness`); `API Reference` → `/api-reference/endpoints` (match `/api-reference`); `Get API Key` → `https://console.mubit.ai` (external CTA).
  - `sidebar` imported from `components/sidebar.ts`.
  - `head()` — inject `/styles/global.css`; keep the optional PostHog block gated on `VITE_PUBLIC_POSTHOG_KEY` (harmless if unset).
  - `vite.resolve.alias` for `@components` and the `usePageData` deep-import alias `PageHeader` needs.
- **`docs-site/tsconfig.json`** — swap Astro's config for the reference's React/Vocs tsconfig.
- **Delete Astro**: `astro.config.mjs`, `src/` (Astro content/assets/styles) — but only *after* content is ported (Step 5).

### Step 3 — Branding assets

- Produce light/dark logo SVGs at `public/images/logo-light.svg` and `public/images/logo-dark.svg` from Minima's existing `src/assets/logo.svg` (its "m" color is already dynamic; fix explicit fills per variant).
- Add `public/images/favicon.png` (or reuse the existing `public/favicon.svg` and point `iconUrl` at it — Vocs accepts SVG).
- Keep the wordmark as **Minima** (this is the Minima product's docs); theming/layout matches the reference.

### Step 4 — Navigation & sidebar structure

`components/sidebar.ts`, path-keyed:

- **`/sdk`**: *Start Here* → Getting Started, Concepts · *Reference* → Python Client SDK · *Guides* → Examples
- **`/harness`**: Overview · Installation · Configuration · Interactive TUI · CLI Usage · Tools & Permissions · Model Routing · Sessions · Troubleshooting
- **`/api-reference`**: API Reference (endpoints)

### Step 5 — Port existing SDK content (Starlight → Vocs)

Move each existing page from `src/content/docs/*.mdx` to `pages/` and swap the component layer:

| From (Starlight) | To (Vocs) |
| --- | --- |
| `index.mdx` | `pages/index.mdx` (home — "what is Minima" + two ways to use: SDK / Harness CLI cards) |
| `getting-started.mdx` | `pages/sdk/getting-started.mdx` |
| `concepts.mdx` | `pages/sdk/concepts.mdx` |
| `client-sdk.mdx` | `pages/sdk/client-sdk.mdx` |
| `examples.mdx` | `pages/sdk/examples.mdx` |
| `api-reference.mdx` | `pages/api-reference/endpoints.mdx` |

Component/import migration (mechanical, applied to every page):
- Replace Starlight import lines with `import { ... } from '@components'`; add `<PageHeader />` after frontmatter (reference convention).
- `CardGrid`→`CardGroup`; `Aside` (`note`/`tip`/`caution`/`danger`)→`Note`/`Tip`/`Warning`; `Steps`→`Steps`+`Step`; `Tabs`/`TabItem`→`Tabs`/`Tab`; `Badge`→ new `Badge`; `<Code>`→ fenced code blocks with ` ```lang title="..." `.
- Keep prose, tables, and code content unchanged — only the wrapping components change.

### Step 6 — Author the Harness CLI section (`pages/harness/*.mdx`)

New content derived from `packages/tui/` (`src/cli/main.ts`, `src/tui/app.tsx`, `src/tui/config_store.ts`, `README.md`):

- **`overview.mdx`** — what the `minima` harness is: a cost-aware, memory-isolated terminal coding agent that routes each prompt through the Minima recommender, runs the chosen model itself (never proxies), executes tools with permission gating, and feeds outcomes back.
- **`installation.mdx`** — prerequisites (Bun ≥ 1.2, `MUBIT_API_KEY`, ≥1 provider key); `bun install` → `bun run build` → `./dist/minima`; first-run `minima auth` (browser login + per-repo project provisioning) vs. manual `minima config set`.
- **`configuration.mdx`** — env vars (`MUBIT_API_KEY`, `MINIMA_URL`, `MINIMA_API_KEY`, provider keys, `MINIMA_NAMESPACE`, `MINIMA_TIMEOUT`); config precedence (project `.env`/`.env.harness` > shell env > user store); credential storage (keychain via `keytar`, fallback `~/.minima-harness/config.env`); supported providers + default model catalog.
- **`interactive.mdx`** — starting/quitting the TUI; status bar; keyboard shortcuts (Ctrl+L picker, Ctrl+P palette, Ctrl+R route mode, scroll keys, Ctrl+C abort/quit); slash commands (`/model`, `/clear`, `/help`, `/cost`, `/thoughts`, `/judge`, `/mouse`, `/name`, `/session`, …).
- **`cli.mdx`** — non-interactive modes: one-shot `minima -p`, JSON event stream `minima --mode json`, flags (`--model`, `--provider`, `--thinking`, `--offline`, `-t/-xt/-nt`), and the `minima auth` / `minima config [set|get]` subcommands.
- **`tools.mdx`** — built-in tools (`read`, `write`, `edit`, `bash`, `ls`, `glob`, `grep`, `web_fetch`, `todowrite`); the permission model (reads auto-approved by directory; writes/bash prompt; grant-always); `/perms`, `/undo`, and `/plan` read-only mode.
- **`routing.mdx`** — how routing works: route → run → judge → feedback loop, cost/quality slider intuition, pinning a model vs. `auto`, `--offline` fallback. Cross-link to the SDK Concepts page.
- **`sessions.mdx`** — append-only session tree in `~/.minima-harness/sessions/`; `/new`, `/resume`, `/fork`, `/clone`, `/tree`, `/compact`.
- **`troubleshooting.mdx`** — `/reconnect`, auth failures (`minima auth`), permission denials, context overflow (`/compact`), missing provider keys.

### Step 7 — Deploy config

- **`Dockerfile`** — adapt to the reference's two-stage pattern: `node:22-alpine` builder (`npm ci` → `npx patch-package` → `node scripts/generate-md.mjs` → `vocs build`) → `nginx:1.27-alpine` serving `dist/` with a `$PORT`-templated conf and SPA fallback (`try_files $uri $uri.html $uri/ /index.html`) + `/healthz`.
- **`nginx.conf`** — update for the Vocs `dist/` layout and SPA fallback (drop the `/_astro/*` rule; long-cache static assets; no-cache HTML).
- **`netlify.toml`** — unchanged in spirit: build `npm run build`, publish `dist/`, SPA redirect `/* → /index.html 200`.

---

## Critical files

**Create / heavily adapt (from reference):**
- `docs-site/vocs.config.ts`, `docs-site/tsconfig.json`, `docs-site/package.json`
- `docs-site/components/` (all listed components + `index.ts` + `sidebar.ts` + new `Badge`)
- `docs-site/patches/vocs+1.4.1.patch`, `docs-site/scripts/generate-md.mjs`

133	export function getSectionForPath(path: string): string | undefined {
134	  const normalized = path.replace(/\/+$/, '') || '/'
135	
136	  const orderedKeys = [
137	    ...Object.keys(sidebar).filter((k) => k !== '/' && (normalized === k || normalized.startsWith(`${k}/`))),
138	    '/',
139	    ...Object.keys(sidebar).filter((k) => k !== '/' && !(normalized === k || normalized.startsWith(`${k}/`))),
140	  ]
141	
142	  for (const key of orderedKeys) {
143	    for (const group of sidebar[key] ?? []) {
144	      for (const item of group.items) {
145	        if (item.link === normalized) return group.text
146	        if (item.items?.some((nested) => nested.link === normalized)) return group.text
147	      }
148	    }
149	  }
150	  return undefined
151	}
