1	// Generates plain-markdown twins of every .mdx page into public/, so the
2	// "Copy as Markdown" button can fetch them at runtime.
3	//
4	// JSX components are converted to readable markdown:
5	//   <PageHeader/>          -> stripped
6	//   <Tabs><Tab title=X>..  -> #### X heading per tab
7	//   <Steps><Step title=..  -> numbered list
8	//   <CardGroup><Card ..>   -> bullet list with title + href
9	//   <Note>/<Warning>/<Tip> -> blockquote
10	//   <Accordion title=X>    -> ### X + body
11	//   <Frame caption=X>      -> figure body + italic caption
12	//   <MubitTerminal/>       -> "live demo" note
13	//   any other JSX tag      -> tag stripped, inner content preserved
14	//
15	// Code (fenced + inline backticks) is protected with an alphanumeric token
16	// before transformation and restored at the end so contents aren't mangled.
17	
18	import fs from 'node:fs/promises'
19	import path from 'node:path'
20	import { fileURLToPath } from 'node:url'
21	
22	const HERE = path.dirname(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url))
23	const ROOT = path.resolve(HERE, '..')
24	const PAGES_DIR = path.join(ROOT, 'pages')
25	const OUT_DIR = path.join(ROOT, 'public')
26	
27	function parseAttrs(attrString) {
28	  const attrs = {}
29	  if (!attrString) return attrs
30	  const re =
31	    /(\w+)\s*=\s*(?:"([^"]*)"|'([^']*)'|\{(?:"([^"]*)"|'([^']*)'|([^}]+))\})/g
32	  let m
33	  while ((m = re.exec(attrString))) {
34	    attrs[m[1]] = m[2] ?? m[3] ?? m[4] ?? m[5] ?? (m[6] ?? '').trim()
35	  }
36	  return attrs
37	}
38	
39	function findClosingTag(src, tagName, openEnd) {
40	  const openRe = new RegExp('<' + tagName + '(?:\\s[^>]*)?>', 'g')
41	  const closeRe = new RegExp('</' + tagName + '\\s*>', 'g')
42	  let depth = 1
43	  let pos = openEnd
44	  while (pos < src.length) {
45	    openRe.lastIndex = pos
46	    closeRe.lastIndex = pos
47	    const o = openRe.exec(src)
48	    const c = closeRe.exec(src)
49	    if (!c) return null
50	    if (o && o.index < c.index) {
51	      depth++
52	      pos = o.index + o[0].length
53	    } else {
54	      depth--
55	      if (depth === 0) return { start: c.index, end: c.index + c[0].length }
56	      pos = c.index + c[0].length
57	    }
58	  }
59	  return null
60	}
61	
62	function replaceTag(src, tagName, openTransform, selfTransform) {
63	  if (selfTransform) {
64	    const selfRe = new RegExp('<' + tagName + '((?:\\s[^/>]*)?)\\s*/>', 'g')
65	    src = src.replace(selfRe, (_, attrStr) => selfTransform(parseAttrs(attrStr)))
66	  }
67	  const openRe = new RegExp('<' + tagName + '((?:\\s[^>]*)?)>', 'g')
68	  let result = ''
69	  let lastIdx = 0
70	  let m
71	  while ((m = openRe.exec(src))) {
72	    const openStart = m.index
73	    const openEnd = openStart + m[0].length
74	    const attrs = parseAttrs(m[1])
75	    const close = findClosingTag(src, tagName, openEnd)
76	    if (!close) break
77	    const inner = src.slice(openEnd, close.start)
78	    result += src.slice(lastIdx, openStart)
79	    result += openTransform(attrs, inner)
80	    lastIdx = close.end
81	    openRe.lastIndex = close.end
82	  }
83	  result += src.slice(lastIdx)
84	  return result
85	}
86	
87	function parseFrontmatter(src) {
88	  const m = src.match(/^---\n([\s\S]*?)\n---\n?/)
89	  if (!m) return { meta: {}, body: src }
90	  const meta = {}
91	  for (const line of m[1].split('\n')) {
92	    const idx = line.indexOf(':')
93	    if (idx === -1) continue
94	    const key = line.slice(0, idx).trim()
95	    let value = line.slice(idx + 1).trim()
96	    value = value.replace(/^['"]|['"]$/g, '')
97	    if (key) meta[key] = value
98	  }
99	  return { meta, body: src.slice(m[0].length) }
100	}
101	
102	function protectCode(src) {
103	  const blocks = []
104	  function tokenFor(match) {
105	    const i = blocks.length
106	    blocks.push(match)
107	    return 'XMDCODE' + i + 'ZZ'
108	  }
109	  let out = src
110	  out = out.replace(/```[\s\S]*?```/g, tokenFor)
111	  out = out.replace(/`[^`\n]+`/g, tokenFor)
112	  return { protectedSrc: out, blocks }
113	}
114	
115	function restoreCode(src, blocks) {
116	  return src.replace(/XMDCODE(\d+)ZZ/g, (_, i) => blocks[Number(i)])
117	}
118	
119	function transformBody(body) {
120	  // Protect code FIRST so import-stripping can't reach into code blocks.
121	  const { protectedSrc, blocks } = protectCode(body)
122	  let out = protectedSrc
123	
124	  // Strip ESM imports/exports (single-line only — must not span newlines).
125	  out = out.replace(/^[ \t]*import\s+[^\n]+?from\s+['"][^'"]+['"];?[ \t]*\n/gm, '')
126	  out = out.replace(/^[ \t]*import\s+['"][^'"]+['"];?[ \t]*\n/gm, '')
127	  out = out.replace(/^[ \t]*export\s+[^\n]+;?[ \t]*\n/gm, '')
128	
129	  out = replaceTag(out, 'PageHeader', () => '', () => '')
130	  const terminalNote =
131	    '\n_(Interactive terminal demo — view the live page for the animated walkthrough.)_\n'
132	  out = replaceTag(out, 'MubitTerminal', () => terminalNote, () => terminalNote)
133	
134	  out = replaceTag(out, 'Tabs', (_attrs, inner) => {
135	    const collected = []
136	    replaceTag(inner, 'Tab', (attrs, tabInner) => {
137	      const label = attrs.title || attrs.label || 'Tab'
138	      collected.push('\n#### ' + label + '\n\n' + tabInner.trim() + '\n')
139	      return ''
140	    })
141	    return '\n' + collected.join('\n') + '\n'
142	  })
143	
144	  out = replaceTag(out, 'Steps', (_attrs, inner) => {
145	    const items = []
146	    let i = 0
147	    replaceTag(inner, 'Step', (attrs, stepInner) => {
148	      i++
149	      const head = attrs.title ? '**' + attrs.title + '**\n\n   ' : ''
150	      const lines = stepInner.trim().split('\n').join('\n   ')
151	      items.push(i + '. ' + head + lines)
152	      return ''
153	    })
154	    return '\n' + items.join('\n\n') + '\n'
155	  })
156	
157	  function renderCard(attrs, inner) {
158	    const desc = inner.trim().replace(/\s+/g, ' ')
159	    const title = attrs.title || ''
160	    if (attrs.href) {
161	      return '- **[' + title + '](' + attrs.href + ')** — ' + desc
162	    }
163	    return '- **' + title + '** — ' + desc
164	  }
165	
166	  out = replaceTag(out, 'CardGroup', (_attrs, inner) => {
167	    const cards = []
168	    replaceTag(inner, 'Card', (attrs, cardInner) => {
169	      cards.push(renderCard(attrs, cardInner))
170	      return ''
171	    })
172	    return '\n' + cards.join('\n') + '\n'
173	  })
174	
175	  // Catch any stray <Card> outside a <CardGroup>.
176	  out = replaceTag(out, 'Card', renderCard)
177	
178	  for (const tag of ['Note', 'Warning', 'Tip']) {
179	    out = replaceTag(out, tag, (_attrs, inner) => {
180	      const body = '**' + tag + ':** ' + inner.trim()
181	      const quoted = body.split('\n').map((l) => '> ' + l).join('\n')
182	      return '\n' + quoted + '\n'
183	    })
184	  }
185	
186	  out = replaceTag(out, 'Accordion', (attrs, inner) => {
187	    return '\n### ' + (attrs.title || 'Details') + '\n\n' + inner.trim() + '\n'
188	  })
189	
190	  out = replaceTag(out, 'Frame', (attrs, inner) => {
191	    let body = inner.trim()
192	    if (attrs.caption) body += '\n\n_' + attrs.caption + '_'
193	    return body
194	  })
195	
196	  // Strip remaining JSX wrappers. Iterate until stable for nested wrappers.
197	  let prev
198	  do {
199	    prev = out
200	    out = out.replace(/<[A-Za-z][\w.-]*(?:\s[^>]*)?\/>/g, '')
201	    out = out.replace(/<[A-Za-z][\w.-]*(?:\s[^>]*)?>/g, '')
202	    out = out.replace(/<\/[A-Za-z][\w.-]*\s*>/g, '')
203	  } while (out !== prev)
204	
205	  out = restoreCode(out, blocks)
206	
207	  out = out.replace(/\n{3,}/g, '\n\n')
208	
209	  return out.trim() + '\n'
210	}
211	
212	async function walk(dir) {
213	  const entries = await fs.readdir(dir, { withFileTypes: true })
214	  const files = []
215	  for (const e of entries) {
216	    if (e.name.startsWith('.')) continue
217	    const full = path.join(dir, e.name)
218	    if (e.isDirectory()) files.push(...(await walk(full)))
219	    else if (e.name.endsWith('.mdx') || e.name.endsWith('.md')) files.push(full)
220	  }
221	  return files
222	}
223	
224	async function main() {
225	  let pages
226	  try {
227	    pages = await walk(PAGES_DIR)
228	  } catch (err) {
229	    if (err.code === 'ENOENT') {
230	      console.warn('[generate-md] no pages directory at ' + PAGES_DIR + ', skipping')
231	      return
232	    }
233	    throw err
234	  }
235	
236	  await fs.mkdir(OUT_DIR, { recursive: true })
237	
238	  let written = 0
239	  for (const file of pages) {
240	    const rel = path.relative(PAGES_DIR, file)
241	    const src = await fs.readFile(file, 'utf8')
242	    const { meta, body } = parseFrontmatter(src)
243	    const md = transformBody(body)
244	
245	    const header = []
246	    if (meta.title) header.push('# ' + meta.title)
247	    if (meta.description) header.push('\n> ' + meta.description + '\n')
248	    header.push('<!-- Auto-generated from pages/' + rel + ' — do not edit by hand. -->')
249	
250	    // Strip a duplicate H1 if the body opens with the same title as frontmatter.
251	    let bodyOut = md
252	    if (meta.title) {
253	      const dupRe = new RegExp('^#\\s+' + meta.title.replace(/[.*+?^${}()|[\]\\]/g, '\\$&') + '\\s*\\n', 'm')
254	      bodyOut = bodyOut.replace(dupRe, '')
255	    }
256	
257	    const final = header.join('\n') + '\n\n' + bodyOut
258	    const outRel = rel.replace(/\.(md|mdx)$/, '.md')
259	    const outPath = path.join(OUT_DIR, outRel)
260	    await fs.mkdir(path.dirname(outPath), { recursive: true })
261	    await fs.writeFile(outPath, final, 'utf8')
262	    written++
263	  }
264	  console.log('[generate-md] wrote ' + written + ' .md file(s) into ' + path.relative(ROOT, OUT_DIR) + '/')
265	}

1	# syntax=docker/dockerfile:1.7
2	
3	# ─── Stage 1: build the Vocs static site ──────────────────────────────────────
4	FROM node:22-alpine AS builder
5	WORKDIR /app
6	
7	# Vocs shells out to `git` during the build (last-updated timestamps).
8	RUN apk add --no-cache git
9	
10	# Install deps with cache mount and the committed lockfile.
11	COPY package.json package-lock.json .npmrc ./
12	COPY patches ./patches
13	RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/root/.npm \
14	    npm ci --no-audit --no-fund
15	# `.npmrc` sets `ignore-scripts=true`, which blocks the `postinstall` hook
16	# from auto-running `patch-package`. Invoke it explicitly so our vocs patches
17	# (CopyMarkdownButton outline injection, footer cleanup, search fixes) apply.
18	RUN npx patch-package
19	
20	# Copy the rest and build.
21	COPY . .
22	
23	# PostHog (public project key) — injected at build time so Vite can bake it into
24	# the static bundle. Empty defaults mean a plain `docker build` with no args
25	# still works; it just ships without analytics (the config guards on the key).
26	ARG VITE_PUBLIC_POSTHOG_KEY=""
27	ARG VITE_PUBLIC_POSTHOG_HOST="https://eu.i.posthog.com"
28	ENV VITE_PUBLIC_POSTHOG_KEY=$VITE_PUBLIC_POSTHOG_KEY
29	ENV VITE_PUBLIC_POSTHOG_HOST=$VITE_PUBLIC_POSTHOG_HOST
30	
31	# `.npmrc`'s `ignore-scripts=true` also blocks pre/post hooks of `npm run X`
32	# in some npm versions, so we cannot rely on the `prebuild` hook to convert
33	# .mdx → .md for the "Copy as Markdown" button. Invoke the generator
34	# explicitly so the .md files exist before `vocs build` copies public/ → dist/.
35	RUN node scripts/generate-md.mjs
36	RUN npm run build
37	
38	# ─── Stage 2: serve the static output with nginx ──────────────────────────────
39	FROM nginx:1.27-alpine AS runtime
40	
41	# Cloud Run injects $PORT; nginx defaults to 80 which Cloud Run rejects.
42	ENV PORT=8080
43	
44	# Drop the default site; render ours at container start with $PORT substituted.
45	RUN rm /etc/nginx/conf.d/default.conf
46	COPY nginx.conf.template /etc/nginx/templates/default.conf.template
47	
48	# Copy the built site.
49	COPY --from=builder /app/dist /usr/share/nginx/html
50	
51	EXPOSE 8080
52	
53	# nginx:alpine includes /docker-entrypoint.d/20-envsubst-on-templates.sh which
54	# expands envvars referenced in /etc/nginx/templates/*.template → /etc/nginx/conf.d
55	# before launching nginx. No CMD override needed.

Important discovery: the `patches/vocs+1.4.1.patch` is 1.9 MB and its `Outline.js` patch hard-imports **both** `@components/CopyMarkdownButton` and `@components/DocsChatPanel` — the chat-backend component I'm deliberately skipping — plus hundreds of Mubit-specific `search.index.*.json` cache files. Applying it verbatim would force me to vendor the whole chat feature and would break the build without it.

That patch is Mubit product customization (chat assistant, copy-markdown, their search tuning), not part of the "stack/design/style/framework." **I'll drop the patch, patch-package, generate-md, and the copy-markdown/chat features entirely** — consistent with the approved plan already skipping the backend-dependent components. The site stays a pixel-faithful Vocs site (same theme, fonts, colors, components, layout). I'll flag this deviation at the end.

1	---
2	title: Minima
3	description: A recommendation engine for LLM model routing. Cuts token spend without losing quality.
4	template: splash
5	hero:
6	  tagline: A recommendation engine, not a router. Route every LLM call to the cheapest model that will actually succeed — then watch the picks get sharper.
7	  image:
8	    file: ../../assets/logo.svg
9	  actions:
10	    - text: Get Started
11	      link: /getting-started/
12	      icon: right-arrow
13	    - text: Concepts
14	      link: /concepts/
15	      icon: open-book
16	      variant: minimal
17	---
18	
19	import { Card, CardGrid } from '@astrojs/starlight/components';
20	
21	## How it works
22	
23	<CardGrid>
24	  <Card title="Recommend" icon="magnifier">
25	    `POST /v1/recommend` — Minima recalls similar past `task → model → outcome` records, aggregates empirical success rates, and returns the cheapest model expected to clear your quality bar. Zero added latency to your actual LLM call.
26	  </Card>
27	  <Card title="Run it yourself" icon="rocket">
28	    Minima hands back a `recommended_model` and a `recommendation_id`. You run the model in your own stack — Minima never proxies, executes, or rewrites.
29	  </Card>
30	  <Card title="Feed back" icon="pencil">
31	    `POST /v1/feedback` — report the outcome, quality score, and realized tokens. Minima writes the outcome to memory, reinforces the exact neighbors that drove the pick, and promotes durable lessons.
32	  </Card>
33	  <Card title="Improve automatically" icon="star">
34	    Every feedback call makes the next recommendation sharper. The cost basis climbs from flat estimates → observed median → rescaled per-request as history accumulates.
35	  </Card>
36	</CardGrid>
37	
38	## Key properties
39	
40	- **Recommend-only.** No proxy, no prompt rewriting, no caching. Sits beside your calls, adds zero latency to them.
41	- **Memory-driven.** Backed by [Mubit](https://mubit.ai) — semantic k-NN recall, per-entry reinforcement, and lesson promotion.
42	- **Accurate cost ranking.** Three cost-basis tiers (rescaled → observed → estimate) correctly rank reasoning-heavy models that look cheap on paper but spend output tokens thinking.
43	- **Works cold.** Prior-only fallback + benchmark seeding means useful picks from day one.
44	- **Provable.** `GET /v1/savings` reports estimated *and realized* savings against honest baselines, and `GET /v1/calibration` shows whether `predicted_success` matches your real outcomes.
45	- **Bring your own memory.** Authenticate with your own Mubit API key — Minima reads and writes your `task → model → outcome` history in your Mubit instance, with `namespace` / `user_id` sub-scoping inside it.
46	
47	```
48	  POST /v1/recommend  ──▶  you run the model  ──▶  POST /v1/feedback
49	   (recall + rank)          (your stack)            (write outcome, reinforce)
50	        ▲                                                    │
51	        └────────────── picks get sharper ──────────────────┘
52	```
53

1	---
2	title: Getting Started
3	description: From an API key to a working recommendation and a closed feedback loop in a few minutes.
4	sidebar:
5	  order: 1
6	---
7	
8	import { Steps, Aside } from '@astrojs/starlight/components';
9	
10	Minima is a hosted API. There is nothing to install or run — you call the service with an API key, get a model recommendation, run that model in **your own** stack, and report the outcome so the next pick gets sharper. This walks you from a key to a closed feedback loop.
11	
12	## Prerequisites
13	
14	- **A Mubit API key** (`mbt_…`) — your Mubit data-plane key. Minima passes it through on each request to read and write your `task → model → outcome` history in Mubit; there is no separate Minima key. [Request access](https://minima.sh) if you don't have one yet.
15	- Nothing else. Minima computes its own embeddings server-side — there's no model, database, or runtime for you to operate.
16	
17	<Aside type="note" title="Base URL">
18	All requests go to `https://api.minima.sh`. The examples below read your Mubit key from a `MUBIT_API_KEY` environment variable — `export MUBIT_API_KEY=[REDACTED] before running them.
19	</Aside>
20	
21	<Steps>
22	
23	1. **Make your first recommendation**
24	
25	   ```bash
26	   curl -s https://api.minima.sh/v1/recommend \
27	     -H "authorization: Bearer [REDACTED_BEARER_TOKEN]" \
28	     -H 'content-type: application/json' \
29	     -d '{
30	           "task": {"task": "Summarize this 2-page incident report into 3 bullet points.",
31	                    "task_type": "summarization"},
32	           "cost_quality_tradeoff": 3
33	         }' | jq
34	   ```
35	
36	   You get back a `recommendation_id`, a `recommended_model`, a ranked candidate list, a `fallback_model`, and a `decision_basis` (`memory` | `prior` | `llm`).
37	
38	2. **Run the model yourself**
39	
40	   Minima hands back the pick — it never proxies, executes, or rewrites. Run the recommended model in your own stack with your own provider credentials. Keep the `recommendation_id`; you'll quote it back in the next step.
41	
42	3. **Close the loop**
43	
44	   Tell Minima how it went. This is what makes the next recommendation sharper — and it populates the realized cost/token history that powers accurate cost ranking.
45	
46	   ```bash
47	   curl -s https://api.minima.sh/v1/feedback \
48	     -H "authorization: Bearer [REDACTED_BEARER_TOKEN]" \
49	     -H 'content-type: application/json' \
50	     -d '{
51	           "recommendation_id": "<from step 1>",
52	           "chosen_model_id": "claude-haiku-4-5",
53	           "outcome": "success",
54	           "quality_score": 0.95,
55	           "input_tokens": 1180,
56	           "output_tokens": 320,
57	           "actual_cost_usd": 0.0028,
58	           "verified_in_production": true
59	         }' | jq
60	   ```
61	
62	</Steps>
63	
64	<Aside type="tip" title="Cold start">
65	On day one your account has little history, so early picks lean on capability priors (`decision_basis: "prior"`, a `cold_start` warning) and the cheap-LLM reasoner fires more often. As your `/v1/feedback` outcomes accumulate, recommendations shift to `decision_basis: "memory"` and the cost ranking sharpens automatically — see [How it gets better over time](/concepts/#how-it-gets-better-over-time).
66	</Aside>
67	
68	## Next steps
69	
70	- Use the typed [Python Client SDK](/client-sdk/) instead of raw `curl`.
71	- Read [Concepts](/concepts/) to understand the slider, the cost-basis tiers, and the escalation path.
72	- Browse the [Examples](/examples/) for constraints, workflows, and a production routing wrapper.
73

1	---
2	title: Concepts
3	description: The loop, the algorithm, cost-basis tiers, escalation, and how Minima improves over time.
4	sidebar:
5	  order: 2
6	---
7	
8	import { Aside, Tabs, TabItem } from '@astrojs/starlight/components';
9	
10	## The problem Minima solves
11	
12	LLM workflows overspend by sending every call to a top-tier model when a cheaper model would do a portion of the work just as well. Token cost is the lever; **model choice is the cheapest knob to turn**. Minima turns that knob, per task, based on what models have actually done on similar tasks before.
13	
14	## Recommend-only, zero added latency
15	
16	Minima **only recommends**. It does not proxy your call, execute a model, rewrite prompts, cache, or compress. You ask "which model should run this?", it answers, and you run the model yourself in your own stack. Because Minima sits *beside* your call rather than in front of it, it adds **zero latency to the actual LLM request**. The only Minima round-trip is the recommendation lookup (typically ~100–300ms).
17	
18	## The loop
19	
20	```
21	        ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
22	        │                                                           │
23	        ▼                                                           │
24	  POST /v1/recommend  ──▶  you run the model  ──▶  POST /v1/feedback
25	   (recall + rank)          (your stack)            (write outcome,
26	                                                     reinforce memory)
27	        ▲                                                           │
28	        │              memory gets sharper for next time           │
29	        └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────-─┘
30	```
31	
32	1. **Recommend.** Minima recalls similar past `task → model → outcome` records from Mubit, aggregates each candidate model's empirical success rate, combines it with cost and capability priors, and returns the cheapest model expected to clear a quality bar.
33	2. **Run it yourself.** Minima hands back a `recommendation_id`; you run the recommended model.
34	3. **Feed back.** You report the outcome and a quality score. Minima writes the outcome to Mubit, reinforces the exact memories that drove the decision, and (on strong verified-in-production results) promotes a durable lesson.
35	
36	## Why memory
37	
38	The recommendation engine is **non-parametric k-NN over history**: recall similar past records, aggregate per-model success, pick the cheapest model clearing a threshold. Minima is backed by [Mubit](https://mubit.ai), which provides that substrate — semantic recall over server-side embeddings, per-entry reinforcement with a Bayesian reliability estimate, lesson promotion, and strategy surfacing for explainability. You don't operate any of it; it's part of the hosted service.
39	
40	## The recommendation algorithm
41	
42	For each request Minima:
43	
44	1. **Classifies** the task. It uses your `task_type` / `difficulty` hints if given; otherwise a fast heuristic infers them. If the heuristic is uncertain and escalation is allowed, the cheap-LLM reasoner can refine the classification. From this it derives a *task cluster* (e.g. `code:hard`).
45	
46	2. **Selects candidates.** It starts from the full model catalog, applies your constraint filters (`candidate_models`, `allowed_providers`, `excluded_models`, `require_prompt_caching`, `require_context_window`), pre-ranks by capability prior, and caps to `max_candidates`.
47	
48	3. **Recalls** similar past outcome records scoped to your account (and `namespace`, if set), with a hard timeout. On timeout or no history, it falls back to the prior-only path.
49	
50	4. **Aggregates per model.** Each recalled neighbor is weighted by `similarity × reliability × staleness_decay`, then combined into a Beta-smoothed empirical success rate per candidate (so models with no neighbors fall back to their capability prior, not to 0.5). An inverse-propensity weighting step corrects for the selection bias that you've historically sent certain task types to certain models.
51	
52	5. **Scores** each candidate by combining predicted success with estimated cost (see **Cost-basis tiers** below). The slider sets a quality threshold `τ`.
53	
54	6. **Optimizes.** Among models predicted to clear `τ`, it recommends the **cheapest** (tie-break: higher success, then higher confidence). If none clear `τ`, it recommends the highest-predicted-success model and warns `no_model_meets_threshold`. A `fallback_model` is chosen as a more reliable retry target.
55	
56	7. **Escalates** to a cheap-LLM reasoner when evidence is thin or conflicting — see below.
57	
58	## The cost/quality slider
59	
60	`cost_quality_tradeoff` (0–10, default 5) maps to a quality threshold:
61	
62	```
63	τ = τ_min + (cost_quality_tradeoff / 10) × (τ_max − τ_min)
64	```
65	
66	with `τ_min = 0.55` and `τ_max = 0.92` by default. **0 means "cheapest model that's acceptable"; 10 means "highest quality regardless of cost".** A request's `min_quality` constraint raises the floor. The slider also shifts the ranking weight between predicted success and normalized cost.
67	
68	## Cost-basis tiers (estimate → observed → rescaled)
69	
70	The single most important accuracy mechanism. A flat token estimate assumes a fixed output length, so it **ignores reasoning/thinking tokens** — which mis-ranks a model with cheap list prices but heavy internal reasoning. Minima ranks candidates by what they *really* cost.
71	
72	One basis is chosen for the **whole candidate set** so all costs are compared like-for-like (`choose_cost_basis`), preferring the most grounded tier every candidate supports:
73	
74	| Tier | Used when | How cost is computed | Breakdown key |
75	|------|-----------|----------------------|---------------|
76	| **rescaled** | every candidate has enough observations carrying `output_tokens` | `this_request_input_tokens × input_price + observed_median_output_tokens × output_price` — size-exact **and** reasoning-aware | `rescaled`, `obs_output_tokens` |
77	| **observed** | every candidate has enough realized `cost_usd` observations | robust similarity-weighted **median** of realized `cost_usd` per call | `observed_avg` |
78	| **estimate** | cold start | `input_tokens × input_price + output_tokens × output_price`, using the request's expected tokens or per-task-type defaults | `input`, `output` |
79	
80	A small minimum number of observations per candidate (default 3) gates the observed and rescaled tiers. The chosen basis is reflected in each `RankedModel.est_cost_breakdown`, and the rationale tags the number `obs` (grounded) or `est` (cold). The realized `cost_usd` / `input_tokens` / `output_tokens` come from your `POST /v1/feedback` calls — so the more you feed back, the more the ranking climbs from estimate → observed → rescaled.
81	
82	<Aside type="tip">
83	The **median** (not mean) makes the observed/rescaled tiers robust to outlier calls. The weight is similarity-only (not staleness-decayed) because cost is an objective fact about a model, not a quality signal that should fade.
84	</Aside>
85	
86	## Escalation to a cheap-LLM reasoner
87	
88	When deterministic evidence is thin or conflicting, Minima can consult a cheap LLM (an inexpensive reasoning model such as Anthropic Haiku or Gemini Flash). It fires only when `allow_llm_escalation` is true **and** any of:
89	
90	- **thin evidence** — too little recalled history overall, or too few candidate models with any neighbor;
91	- **low confidence** — the recommended model's neighborhood confidence is too low;
92	- **conflict/tie** — the top two candidates' scores are within a small margin.
93	
94	On trigger, Minima builds a memory context block, asks the reasoner to rank the candidates with structured output, and **blends** the reasoner's predicted success with the deterministic one. On any reasoner error or parse failure it falls back to the deterministic result and warns `reasoner_failed`. The reasoner is the explicit slow tier and never touches your real LLM call.
95	
96	`decision_basis` on the response tells you which path won: `memory`, `prior`, or `llm`.
97	
98	## How it gets better over time
99	
100	| Phase | What's happening | Typical `decision_basis` |
101	|-------|------------------|--------------------------|
102	| **Cold start (day 0)** | no history; leans on capability priors and flat estimates; reasoner fires often | `prior` (with `cold_start`) |
103	| **Warming up** | `/feedback` outcomes cross `MIN_N`; cost basis climbs estimate → observed → rescaled; reasoner fires less | mix of `memory` and `prior` |
104	| **Mature** | dense history; most picks are empirical; reflection has promoted durable lessons; selection bias in your routing history has been corrected | mostly `memory` |
105	
106	New accounts start with a baseline of benchmark-derived history so picks are useful from day one; it's progressively dominated by your own `/v1/feedback` outcomes as they accumulate — seeded evidence is explicitly down-weighted and crowded out as live outcomes arrive, and every outcome's influence decays with age (a result from last month counts less than one from yesterday), so the recommender tracks how models behave **now**.
107	
108	## Measuring it
109	
110	Every recommendation is logged with its counterfactual cost baselines and reconciled with the outcome you report — so the two questions that matter are answerable from your own traffic, not from a benchmark:
111	
112	- **Did it save money?** [`GET /v1/savings`](/api-reference/#get-v1savings) reports estimated and realized savings against two explicitly-labeled baselines: the most expensive scored candidate (generous) and your own declared default (`baseline_model_id` — honest). It also reports `feedback_coverage`, the share of recommendations that ever received feedback — the number that tells you how much weight the realized figures can bear.
113	- **Are the predictions honest?** [`GET /v1/calibration`](/api-reference/#get-v1calibration) compares `predicted_success` at decision time against realized outcomes (expected calibration error per task type) and flags sustained drift per `(cluster, model)` — the early-warning signal for a provider silently changing a model.
114	
115	Accounts can additionally opt into a small amount of bounded **exploration** (`selection_policy: epsilon_softmax`): a few percent of picks sample from the *eligible* set (never below your quality bar) instead of always taking the cheapest eligible model, which keeps evidence flowing for under-tried cheap models and makes the logged history statistically sound for off-policy analysis. Default is off — deterministic picks everywhere.
116	
117	## The learning loop in detail
118	
119	When `POST /v1/feedback` is called:
120	
121	1. Resolve the `recommendation_id` → the recalled neighbors, cluster, and scope. (Account-scoped: an id from another account resolves to nothing, so accounts can't credit or poison each other.)
122	2. Upsert one durable **outcome record** per `(task cluster, model)`, carrying `cost_usd`, `input_tokens`, `output_tokens`, and `quality_score`.
123	3. Credit the exact recalled neighbors that drove the pick, bumping their reinforcement counters and reliability.
124	4. On a verified-in-production strong success, promote a durable **lesson** that feeds rule promotion.
125	5. Periodically reflect (after a number of feedbacks, or on any verified-prod failure) to promote run → session → account-level lessons.
126	
127	## Degradation behavior
128	
129	Minima is designed to keep serving when Mubit is slow or down:
130	
131	| Condition | Behavior |
132	|-----------|----------|
133	| Recall timeout | Prior-only recommendation + `recall_timeout` warning. |
134	| Mubit unavailable | Prior-only + `memory_unavailable`. |
135	| Stale prices | Last-good price snapshot used; `catalog_stale: true` + `prices_stale`. |
136	| Reasoner error | Deterministic result + `reasoner_failed`. |
137	| Reasoner not configured | Escalation surfaced as warning; deterministic result used. |
138	| No models match constraints | `422 NoCandidatesError`. |
139

[tool_result] toolu_015gpDxAV72fa1FnW1bWR56t
1	---
2	title: API Reference
3	description: Every Minima endpoint — full request and response schemas, warnings, and error formats.
4	sidebar:
5	  order: 3
6	---
7	
8	import { Aside, Badge } from '@astrojs/starlight/components';
9	
10	Base URL: `https://api.minima.sh`. Base path: `/v1`. All request and response bodies are JSON. Interactive OpenAPI docs are served at [`https://api.minima.sh/docs`](https://api.minima.sh/docs).
11	
12	## Authentication
13	
14	Minima uses **pass-through auth**: present your own **Mubit API key** as a bearer token. There is no separate Minima key — Minima forwards the key to Mubit to read and write your `task → model → outcome` history on your behalf.
15	
16	```
17	Authorization: Bearer [REDACTED_BEARER_TOKEN]
18	```
19	
20	A missing or invalid key returns `401`. Your Mubit key determines which Mubit instance Minima reads and writes — it's your data boundary. `GET /v1/health` is the only endpoint that works without a key (it returns service liveness only).
21	
22	`user_id` and `namespace` are scoping fields **within your Mubit instance**, not auth boundaries — use them to partition recall and learning across teams, projects, or environments.
23	
24	## Errors
25	
26	Errors are returned as `application/problem+json` (RFC 7807-style):
27	
28	```json
29	{ "type": "about:blank", "title": "No candidate models", "status": 422,
30	  "detail": "no models match the supplied constraints" }
31	```
32	
33	| Status | Title | When |
34	|--------|-------|------|
35	| `400` | Invalid request | Request body fails validation. |
36	| `401` | Unauthorized | Missing or invalid Mubit key. |
37	| `422` | No candidate models | Constraints eliminated every catalog model. |
38	
39	<Aside type="note">
40	`POST /v1/feedback` does **not** error on an unknown `recommendation_id`. It returns `200` with `accepted: false` and an `unknown_recommendation` warning, so retried or cross-instance feedback fails safely.
41	</Aside>
42	
43	---
44	
45	## `POST /v1/recommend` <Badge text="core" variant="success" />
46	
47	Recommend a model for a single task.
48	
49	### Request — `RecommendRequest`
50	
51	| Field | Type | Default | Notes |
52	|-------|------|---------|-------|
53	| `task` | `TaskInput` | required | The task to route (see below). |
54	| `cost_quality_tradeoff` | float `0–10` | `5.0` | 0 = cheapest acceptable, 10 = highest quality. Sets the quality threshold `τ`. |
55	| `constraints` | `Constraints` | `{}` | Hard limits on the candidate set (see below). |
56	| `user_id` | string \| null | `null` | Within-account actor label. Scopes recall. |
57	| `namespace` | string \| null | `null` | Within-account sub-scope (team / project / environment). Isolates recall and learning. |
58	| `max_candidates` | int `1–64` | `8` | Cap on candidates considered. |
59	| `allow_llm_escalation` | bool | `true` | Allow the cheap-LLM reasoner when evidence is thin. |
60	| `explain` | bool | `true` | Include `evidence[]` refs on each ranked model. |
61	| `baseline_model_id` | string \| null | `null` | The model you would have used without Minima. Powers the honest `vs_declared` baseline in [`GET /v1/savings`](#get-v1savings). |
62	
63	#### `TaskInput`
64	
65	| Field | Type | Default | Notes |
66	|-------|------|---------|-------|
67	| `task` | string | required | Raw task/prompt text; embedded by Mubit for recall. |
68	| `task_type` | enum \| null | `null` | `code` \| `summarization` \| `extraction` \| `qa` \| `reasoning` \| `classification` \| `translation` \| `creative` \| `rag` \| `tool_use` \| `other`. Heuristic-classified if omitted. |
69	| `difficulty` | enum \| null | `null` | `trivial` \| `easy` \| `medium` \| `hard` \| `expert`. |
70	| `expected_input_tokens` | int ≥ 0 \| null | `null` | Feeds the cost estimate; a per-task-type default is used if omitted. |
71	| `expected_output_tokens` | int ≥ 0 \| null | `null` | Feeds the cost estimate. |
72	| `tags` | string[] | `[]` | Propagated to Mubit `env_tags` (e.g. `lang:python`) for version-aware recall. |
73	
74	#### `Constraints` (all optional)
75	
76	| Field | Type | Notes |
77	|-------|------|-------|
78	| `allowed_providers` | string[] \| null | Whitelist by provider. |
79	| `candidate_models` | string[] \| null | Restrict to these model ids. |
80	| `excluded_models` | string[] \| null | Blacklist by model id. |
81	| `max_cost_per_call` | float ≥ 0 \| null | USD hard filter on estimated cost. |
82	| `min_quality` | float `0–1` \| null | Predicted-success floor; raises `τ`. |
83	| `require_prompt_caching` | bool | Keep only models that support prompt caching. |
84	| `max_latency_ms` | int > 0 \| null | Drop candidates whose **observed** latency (p75 from similar past outcomes) exceeds this. A model with no latency history is never excluded. |
85	| `require_context_window` | int > 0 \| null | Keep only models with at least this context window. |
86	
87	### Response — `RecommendResponse`
88	
89	| Field | Type | Notes |
90	|-------|------|-------|
91	| `recommendation_id` | string | Quote this back to `POST /v1/feedback`. |
92	| `recommended_model` | `RankedModel` | The chosen model. |
93	| `ranked` | `RankedModel[]` | Every candidate, sorted by final score. |
94	| `fallback_model` | `RankedModel` \| null | A more reliable retry target. |
95	| `confidence` | float `0–1` | Overall confidence in the pick. |
96	| `decision_basis` | enum | `memory` \| `prior` \| `llm` — which path produced the pick. |
97	| `threshold_used` | float | The quality threshold `τ` applied. |
98	| `classified_task_type` | enum | Final task type used. |
99	| `classified_difficulty` | enum | Final difficulty used. |
100	| `catalog_version` | string | Catalog version that priced the candidates. |
101	| `catalog_stale` | bool | Prices older than the staleness window. |
102	| `latency_ms` | int | Minima-side recommendation latency. |
103	| `warnings` | string[] | See **Warnings** below. |
104	| `selection_policy` | enum | `argmin` (deterministic cheapest-eligible, the default) \| `epsilon_softmax` (account opted into bounded exploration within the eligible set). |
105	
106	#### `RankedModel`
107	
108	| Field | Type | Notes |
109	|-------|------|-------|
110	| `model_id` | string | |
111	| `provider` | string | |
112	| `predicted_success` | float `0–1` | Probability the model clears the task. |
113	| `est_cost_usd` | float ≥ 0 | Estimated cost for this request, per the chosen cost basis. |
114	| `est_cost_breakdown` | object | Keys depend on the basis: `{rescaled, obs_output_tokens}`, `{observed_avg}`, or `{input, output}`. See [Cost-basis tiers](/concepts/#cost-basis-tiers-estimate--observed--rescaled). |
115	| `score` | float | Final objective score; the sorting key. |
116	| `rationale` | string | Human-readable reason (tags cost as `obs` or `est`). |
117	| `decision_basis` | enum | Per-model basis: `memory` \| `prior` \| `llm`. |
118	| `evidence` | `EvidenceRef[]` | Recalled neighbors that informed this candidate (empty if `explain=false`). |
119	| `supports_prompt_caching` | bool | |
120	| `context_window` | int | |
121	| `est_latency_ms` | float \| null | Observed latency percentile from similar past outcomes; `null` without latency history. |
122	| `latency_basis` | string | e.g. `observed_p75`; empty without latency history. |
123	
124	#### `EvidenceRef`
125	
126	| Field | Type | Notes |
127	|-------|------|-------|
128	| `entry_id` | string | Mubit `QueryEvidence.id` (used for outcome attribution). |
129	| `reference_id` | string \| null | Stable reference id. |
130	| `model_id` | string | The model this past outcome was about. |
131	| `score` | float | Retrieval similarity. |
132	| `knowledge_confidence` | float `0–1` | Mubit's reliability estimate for the entry. |
133	| `observed_success` | float `0–1` | The recorded quality of that past outcome. |
134	| `is_stale` | bool | Whether the entry is marked stale. |
135	
136	### Warnings
137	
138	| Warning | Meaning |
139	|---------|---------|
140	| `cold_start` | No recalled outcomes; prior-only. |
141	| `recall_timeout` | Mubit recall exceeded the timeout; prior-only. |
142	| `memory_unavailable` | Recall errored; prior-only. |
143	| `prices_stale` | Catalog prices older than the staleness window. |
144	| `no_model_meets_threshold` | No candidate cleared `τ`; recommended the highest-success one. |
145	| `no_model_within_cost_budget` | `max_cost_per_call` eliminated all; constraint relaxed for ranking. |
146	| `no_model_within_latency_budget` | `max_latency_ms` eliminated every candidate with latency history; constraint relaxed for ranking. |
147	| `escalation_suggested:<reason>` | Escalation criteria met (`thin_evidence`, `low_confidence`, `tie`, `conflict`, `wide_interval`). |
148	| `exploration_pick` | The opt-in exploration policy sampled a different eligible model than the deterministic pick. |
149	| `reasoner_consulted` | The cheap-LLM reasoner was consulted and changed scores. |
150	| `reasoner_failed` | The reasoner errored or returned unusable output; deterministic result used. |
151	| `reasoner_disabled` | Escalation suggested but no reasoner is configured. |
152	| `llm_classified` | The reasoner refined an ambiguous task classification. |
153	
154	### Example
155	
156	```bash
157	curl -s https://api.minima.sh/v1/recommend \
158	  -H "authorization: Bearer [REDACTED_BEARER_TOKEN]" \
159	  -H 'content-type: application/json' -d '{
160	  "task": {"task": "Write a Python function that merges k sorted linked lists.",
161	           "task_type": "code", "difficulty": "hard",
162	           "expected_input_tokens": 180, "expected_output_tokens": 600,
163	           "tags": ["lang:python"]},
164	  "cost_quality_tradeoff": 3,
165	  "constraints": {"min_quality": 0.8, "excluded_models": ["some-deprecated-model"]},
166	  "namespace": "team-payments"
167	}' | jq
168	```
169	
170	---
171	
172	## `POST /v1/recommend/workflow` <Badge text="workflow" variant="note" />
173	
174	Recommend a model for each step of a multi-step workflow. Each step runs the same engine independently and gets its own `recommendation_id` for per-step feedback.
175	
176	### Request — `WorkflowRequest`
177	
178	| Field | Type | Default | Notes |
179	|-------|------|---------|-------|
180	| `steps` | `WorkflowStep[]` | required | The steps to route. |
181	| `cost_quality_tradeoff` | float `0–10` | `5.0` | Applied to every step. |
182	| `constraints` | `Constraints` | `{}` | Global constraints; each step may override. |
183	| `user_id` | string \| null | `null` | |
184	| `namespace` | string \| null | `null` | |
185	| `allow_llm_escalation` | bool | `true` | |
186	
187	#### `WorkflowStep`
188	
189	| Field | Type | Notes |
190	|-------|------|-------|
191	| `step_id` | string | Caller-defined id (echoed in the response). |
192	| `task` | `TaskInput` | The step's task. |
193	| `constraints` | `Constraints` \| null | Per-step override, **merged over** the global constraints. |
194	| `depends_on` | string[] | Declared dependencies (informational; steps are scored independently). |
195	
196	### Response — `WorkflowResponse`
197	
198	| Field | Type | Notes |
199	|-------|------|-------|
200	| `workflow_recommendation_id` | string | Id for the whole workflow. |
201	| `steps` | `StepRecommendation[]` | `{step_id, recommendation: RecommendResponse}` per step. |
202	| `total_est_cost_usd` | float | Sum of recommended-model costs across steps. |
203	| `total_est_cost_if_all_premium` | float | Sum if each step used its most expensive candidate — the savings baseline. |
204	| `confidence` | float `0–1` | Mean step confidence. |
205	
206	See the [multi-step workflow example](/examples/#4-multi-step-workflow).
207	
208	---
209	
210	## `POST /v1/feedback` <Badge text="core" variant="success" />
211	
212	Report an outcome and close the learning loop. This reinforces the memories that drove the recommendation **and** records realized cost/token history that powers the observed and rescaled cost-basis tiers.
213	
214	### Request — `FeedbackRequest`
215	
216	| Field | Type | Default | Notes |
217	|-------|------|---------|-------|
218	| `recommendation_id` | string | required | From a prior `/recommend` (or a workflow step). |
219	| `chosen_model_id` | string | required | The model you **actually ran** (may differ from the recommendation). |
220	| `outcome` | enum | required | `success` \| `partial` \| `failure`. |
221	| `quality_score` | float `0–1` \| null | `null` | Caller-supplied; no LLM judge. Defaults applied per outcome if omitted (0.9 / 0.5 / 0.1). A score that flatly contradicts the outcome label (e.g. `failure` at `0.95`) is clamped into a consistent band with a `quality_outcome_mismatch` warning. |
222	| `input_tokens` | int ≥ 0 \| null | `null` | Realized input tokens — **populate this** to enable the rescaled cost tier. |
223	| `output_tokens` | int ≥ 0 \| null | `null` | Realized output tokens (captures reasoning/thinking) — **populate this** for the rescaled tier. |
224	| `actual_cost_usd` | float ≥ 0 \| null | `null` | Realized $/call — enables the observed cost tier. |
225	| `latency_ms` | int ≥ 0 \| null | `null` | |
226	| `verified_in_production` | bool | `false` | Marks a real production outcome; gates lesson promotion. |
227	| `notes` | string \| null | `null` | |
228	| `idempotency_key` | string \| null | `null` | Dedupe key; derived from `recommendation_id + model` if omitted. |
229	
230	### Response — `FeedbackResponse`
231	
232	| Field | Type | Notes |
233	|-------|------|-------|
234	| `accepted` | bool | `false` with a warning on failure. |
235	| `record_id` | string \| null | The Mubit id of the upserted outcome record. |
236	| `reinforced_entry_ids` | string[] | The neighbor entry ids credited. |
237	| `updated_confidence` | float \| null | Mubit's updated `knowledge_confidence` for the primary entry. |
238	| `reflection_triggered` | bool | Whether reflection fired this call. |
239	| `lesson_promoted` | bool | Whether a durable lesson was promoted. |
240	| `warnings` | string[] | `unknown_recommendation`, `memory_write_failed`, `reinforcement_failed`, `lesson_promotion_failed`, `quality_outcome_mismatch`, `late_feedback_no_attribution`. |
241	
242	<Aside type="note" title="How late can feedback arrive?">
243	A `recommendation_id` accepts fully-attributed feedback for **7 days**. After that the outcome is still accepted and still teaches the recommender (the durable outcome record is written), but the specific recalled neighbors can no longer be credited — the response carries a `late_feedback_no_attribution` warning.
244	</Aside>
245	
246	### Example
247	
248	```bash
249	curl -s https://api.minima.sh/v1/feedback \
250	  -H "authorization: Bearer [REDACTED_BEARER_TOKEN]" \
251	  -H 'content-type: application/json' -d '{
252	  "recommendation_id": "…",
253	  "chosen_model_id": "claude-haiku-4-5",
254	  "outcome": "success",
255	  "quality_score": 0.95,
256	  "input_tokens": 180, "output_tokens": 640, "actual_cost_usd": 0.0034,
257	  "verified_in_production": true
258	}' | jq
259	```
260	
261	---
262	
263	## `GET /v1/models`
264	
265	The current model catalog (cost + capability priors).
266	
267	### Query parameters
268	
269	| Param | Type | Default | Notes |
270	|-------|------|---------|-------|
271	| `provider` | string | — | Filter by provider (case-insensitive). |
272	| `task_type` | enum | — | Keep only models with a capability prior for this task type. |
273	| `max_cost` | float | — | Keep only models whose max(input, output) $/Mtok ≤ this. |
274	| `include_stale` | bool | `true` | Prefer fresh-priced models when false. |
275	
276	### Response
277	
278	`{ models: ModelCard[], catalog_version, refreshed_at, stale }`, sorted by input price.
279	
280	**`ModelCard`** fields include: `model_id`, `provider`, `display_name`, `input_cost_per_mtok`, `output_cost_per_mtok`, `cache_read_cost_per_mtok`, `supports_prompt_caching`, `context_window`, `max_output_tokens`, `capability_priors`, `capability_by_task_type`, `cost_source`, `cost_fetched_at`, `cost_stale`, `capability_source`.
281	
282	---
283	
284	## `GET /v1/strategies`
285	
286	Surfaces the rules Mubit has promoted for a namespace — the "why" behind routing patterns.
287	
288	### Query parameters
289	
290	| Param | Type | Default | Notes |
291	|-------|------|---------|-------|
292	| `namespace` | string | — | Within-account sub-scope to read strategies for. |
293	| `lesson_types` | string[] | — | Filter by lesson type. |
294	| `max_strategies` | int `1–50` | `5` | |
295	
296	### Response
297	
298	`{ namespace, lane, strategies: Strategy[], count }`, where each `Strategy` has `strategy_id`, `description`, `supporting_lesson_count`, `avg_confidence`, `avg_reinforcement`, `dominant_lesson_type`, `dominant_scope`, `lesson_ids[]`.
299	
300	---
301	
302	## `GET /v1/savings` <Badge text="measurement" variant="tip" />
303	
304	What Minima actually saved you. Every recommendation is logged with its counterfactual cost baselines and reconciled with the realized cost you report to `/v1/feedback` — this endpoint aggregates that ledger for your account.
305	
306	Two baselines are always reported side by side, explicitly labeled: **`vs premium`** (the most expensive candidate that was scored for each request — generous; overstates savings if you'd never have used the premium model) and **`vs declared`** (your own stated default via `baseline_model_id` — honest, but only present on requests where you declared one). Estimated and realized figures are never mixed into one number.
307	
308	### Query parameters
309	
310	| Param | Type | Default | Notes |
311	|-------|------|---------|-------|
312	| `namespace` | string | — | Restrict to one namespace. |
313	| `days` | float `>0–365` | `30` | Lookback window (must be greater than 0). |
314	| `group_by` | enum | — | `cluster` \| `task_type` \| `lane` — optional breakdown. |
315	
316	### Response
317	
318	| Field | Type | Notes |
319	|-------|------|-------|
320	| `summary.estimated` | object | `n`, `cost_recommended_usd`, `cost_premium_usd`, `savings_vs_premium_usd`, plus `n_declared` / `cost_declared_usd` / `savings_vs_declared_usd` over the declared-baseline subset. |
321	| `summary.realized` | object | A **different** field set over the **reconciled** subset (feedback received with `actual_cost_usd`): `n_reconciled`, `realized_cost_usd`, `est_cost_recommended_usd`, `est_cost_premium_usd`, `savings_vs_premium_est_usd`, plus `n_declared` / `est_cost_declared_usd` / `savings_vs_declared_est_usd` — your realized cost measured against the estimated baselines. |
322	| `health` | object | `recommendations`, `feedback_coverage` (share of recommendations that ever got feedback — the number that decides how much to trust `realized`), `late_feedback_share`, `escalation_rate`, `exploration_share`, `epsilon_policy_share`. |
323	| `groups` | array | Per-`group_by` key: the same `summary` + `health`. |
324	
325	```bash
326	curl -s "https://api.minima.sh/v1/savings?days=30&group_by=task_type" \
327	  -H "authorization: Bearer [REDACTED_BEARER_TOKEN]" | jq '.summary, .health'
328	```
329	
330	<Aside type="tip">
331	Send `baseline_model_id` on your `/v1/recommend` calls and realized `actual_cost_usd` on your `/v1/feedback` calls — they turn the savings report from an estimate into a measurement.
332	</Aside>
333	
334	---
335	
336	## `GET /v1/calibration` <Badge text="measurement" variant="tip" />
337	
338	Is `predicted_success` telling the truth for your traffic? Compares each recommendation's predicted success against the realized outcome you reported, per task type.
339	
340	### Query parameters
341	
342	| Param | Type | Default | Notes |
343	|-------|------|---------|-------|
344	| `namespace` | string | — | Restrict to one namespace. |
345	| `days` | float `>0–365` | `30` | Lookback window (must be greater than 0). |
346	
347	### Response
348	
349	| Field | Type | Notes |
350	|-------|------|-------|
351	| `reports` | array | Per slice (first entry is `global`, then each task type): `n`, `ece` (expected calibration error — 0 is perfect), `ece_shrunk` (sparse slices pulled toward the global estimate so 3 feedbacks don't read as a verdict), `ece_quality` (quality-score-weighted variant), and reliability `bins` (`lo`, `hi`, `n`, `avg_predicted`, `avg_realized`). |
352	| `drift_flags` | array | Sustained prediction drift per `(cluster, model)` — `direction` is `over_predicting` (the model got worse than the evidence says; the expensive failure mode) or `under_predicting`. |
353	| `health` | object | Same routing-health rates as `/v1/savings`. |
354	
355	```bash
356	curl -s "https://api.minima.sh/v1/calibration?days=30" \
357	  -H "authorization: Bearer [REDACTED_BEARER_TOKEN]" | jq '.reports[] | {slice_key, n, ece, ece_shrunk}'
358	```
359	
360	---
361	
362	## `GET /v1/health`
363	
364	Always returns `200`; reports degraded state in the body. The only endpoint that doesn't require a key (an unauthenticated probe gets service liveness; a key-bearing probe additionally confirms your account's memory reachability).
365	
366	```json
367	{
368	  "status": "ok",
369	  "mubit": {"reachable": true, "latency_ms": 12, "endpoint": "https://api.mubit.ai", "org_id": "…"},
370	  "auth": "passthrough",
371	  "catalog": {"version": "…", "cost_source": "litellm", "stale": false, "models": 42},
372	  "reasoner": {"provider": "gemini", "configured": true},
373	  "version": "0.4.9"
374	}
375	```
376	
377	An unauthenticated probe (no key) returns `"mubit": {"reachable": null, "scope": "unauthenticated"}` and `status: "ok"` — liveness only. `status` is `degraded` only when a **key-bearing** probe finds your memory backend unreachable (`mubit.reachable == false`); in that state `/recommend` still serves prior-only recommendations.
378

1	---
2	title: Python Client SDK
3	description: The minima_client package — sync and async clients, autocapture, and error handling.
4	sidebar:
5	  order: 5
6	---
7	
8	import { Aside, Code } from '@astrojs/starlight/components';
9	
10	The `minima_client` package is the official thin, typed Python client for the hosted Minima API, plus an optional zero-code intake helper.
11	
12	## Install
13	
14	```bash
15	pip install minima-cli
16	```
17	
18	The SDK is published on PyPI as part of [**`minima-cli`**](https://pypi.org/project/minima-cli/) — one package that ships both the CLI agent and the `minima_client` SDK (also available via `brew install mubit-ai/minima/minima`). Import it as `minima_client`:
19	
20	```python
21	from minima_client import MinimaClient, AsyncMinimaClient, MinimaError, autocapture
22	```
23	
24	## Clients
25	
26	Both `MinimaClient` (sync) and `AsyncMinimaClient` (async) share the same surface.
27	
28	```python
29	from minima_client import MinimaClient
30	
31	with MinimaClient("https://api.minima.sh", api_key="mbt_…", timeout=10.0) as minima:
32	    rec = minima.recommend("Summarize this incident report into 3 bullets.",
33	                           cost_quality_tradeoff=3)
34	    print(rec.recommended_model.model_id)
35	```
36	
37	- `base_url` — the Minima API base URL (`https://api.minima.sh`).
38	- `api_key` — your **Mubit** API key (`mbt_…`), sent as `Authorization: Bearer <key>` and passed through to Mubit. Required.
39	- `timeout` — HTTP timeout in seconds.
40	
41	The async client mirrors every method with `await`; use it inside FastAPI/async apps:
42	
43	```python
44	async with AsyncMinimaClient("https://api.minima.sh", api_key="mbt_…") as minima:
45	    rec = await minima.recommend(task)
46	```
47	
48	## `recommend(task, *, ...)`
49	
50	Returns a `RecommendResponse`.
51	
52	```python
53	rec = minima.recommend(
54	    task,                              # str | TaskInput | dict
55	    cost_quality_tradeoff=5.0,         # 0..10
56	    constraints=None,                  # Constraints | None
57	    user_id=None,
58	    namespace=None,
59	    allow_llm_escalation=True,
60	    explain=True,
61	    baseline_model_id=None,            # the model you'd use without Minima -> savings()
62	)
63	```
64	
65	`task` is flexible:
66	
67	```python
68	minima.recommend("plain prompt text")                       # str
69	minima.recommend({"task": "…", "task_type": "code"})        # dict
70	
71	from minima_client import TaskInput, Constraints
72	minima.recommend(
73	    TaskInput(task="…", difficulty="hard"),
74	    constraints=Constraints(min_quality=0.85, max_cost_per_call=0.02),
75	)
76	```
77	
78	## `recommend_workflow(req)`
79	
80	Takes a `WorkflowRequest`, returns a `WorkflowResponse`.
81	
82	```python
83	from minima_client import WorkflowRequest, WorkflowStep, TaskInput
84	
85	req = WorkflowRequest(
86	    steps=[
87	        WorkflowStep(step_id="extract",
88	                     task=TaskInput(task="Extract entities from …",
89	                                    task_type="extraction")),
90	        WorkflowStep(step_id="reason",
91	                     task=TaskInput(task="Decide next action given …",
92	                                    task_type="reasoning", difficulty="hard")),
93	    ],
94	    cost_quality_tradeoff=4,
95	)
96	wf = minima.recommend_workflow(req)
97	print(wf.total_est_cost_usd, "vs", wf.total_est_cost_if_all_premium)
98	```
99	
100	## `feedback(recommendation_id, chosen_model_id, outcome, **kwargs)`
101	
102	Returns a `FeedbackResponse`. `outcome` is `"success"` | `"partial"` | `"failure"`. Pass realized numbers to power the observed/rescaled cost tiers:
103	
104	```python
105	minima.feedback(
106	    rec.recommendation_id,
107	    rec.recommended_model.model_id,
108	    "success",
109	    quality_score=0.95,
110	    input_tokens=180,
111	    output_tokens=640,
112	    actual_cost_usd=0.0034,
113	    latency_ms=2100,
114	    verified_in_production=True,
115	    idempotency_key="…",  # optional
116	)
117	```
118	
119	## `models(...)`, `strategies(...)`, `health()`
120	
121	```python
122	catalog = minima.models(provider="anthropic", max_cost=10.0)      # ModelsResponse
123	strat   = minima.strategies(namespace="team-payments", max_strategies=5)
124	status  = minima.health()                                          # dict
125	```
126	
127	## `savings(...)` and `calibration(...)`
128	
129	The measurement layer: what Minima saved you, and whether `predicted_success` is calibrated against your reported outcomes. Both report on your account's decision ledger (see the [API reference](/api-reference/#get-v1savings) for every field).
130	
131	```python
132	report = minima.savings(namespace="team-payments", days=30, group_by="task_type")
133	print(report.summary.estimated.savings_vs_premium_usd,   # generous baseline
134	      report.summary.estimated.savings_vs_declared_usd,  # your declared default
135	      report.health["feedback_coverage"])                # how much to trust "realized"
136	
137	cal = minima.calibration(days=30)
138	for r in cal.reports:
139	    print(r.slice_key, r.n, r.ece, r.ece_shrunk)
140	for flag in cal.drift_flags:
141	    print(flag.cluster, flag.model_id, flag.direction)   # sustained prediction drift
142	```
143	
144	Pass `baseline_model_id` on `recommend()` and realized `actual_cost_usd` on `feedback()` to turn the savings report from an estimate into a measurement.
145	
146	## Errors
147	
148	Non-2xx responses raise `MinimaError` (which carries the problem+json detail):
149	
150	```python
151	from minima_client import MinimaError
152	
153	try:
154	    rec = minima.recommend(task)
155	except MinimaError as exc:
156	    # fall back to a default model
157	    ...
158	```
159	
160	---
161	
162	## Zero-code intake: `autocapture`
163	
164	`minima_client.autocapture` is a thin wrapper over `mubit.learn`. Calling `enable()` pins a learn session to the same memory lane Minima recalls from (`minima:<namespace>`) and monkeypatches your OpenAI/Anthropic/LiteLLM/Google-GenAI clients, so every LLM call auto-ingests its trace — no code changes at the call site. Requires `mubit-sdk`.
165	
166	```python
167	from minima_client import autocapture
168	
169	autocapture.enable(
170	    api_key="[REDACTED]",
171	    endpoint="https://api.mubit.ai",
172	    namespace="team-payments",
173	    user_id="svc-router",
174	)
175	
176	# ... your normal OpenAI/Anthropic/LiteLLM calls happen here, auto-captured ...
177	
178	# mubit.learn does NOT fabricate a success signal — close the loop explicitly:
179	autocapture.feedback(good=True)   # or score in [-1, 1]
180	autocapture.disable()             # restore original client behavior
181	```
182	
183	<Aside type="caution" title="What autocapture does and doesn't do">
184	Autocapture lands traces + lessons in Minima's lane (enriching the reasoner's memory block and Mubit's reflection), but it does **not** by itself produce the `kind="outcome"` records the deterministic k-NN aggregator scores. To fully close the loop, either call `autocapture.feedback(...)` or send a quality score to `POST /v1/feedback`.
185	</Aside>
186	
187	Other helpers:
188	
189	```python
190	autocapture.wrap(client)                           # enrich one client instead of global patching

1	---
2	title: Examples
3	description: Copy-paste recipes — from a single curl to a production routing wrapper.
4	sidebar:
5	  order: 3
6	---
7	
8	import { Aside } from '@astrojs/starlight/components';
9	
10	Copy-paste recipes against the hosted API, from a single `curl` to a production routing wrapper. Every snippet is self-contained.
11	
12	<Aside type="tip" title="Setup">
13	Set your key once: `export MUBIT_API_KEY=[REDACTED] The Python examples use the [`minima_client`](/client-sdk/) package and read the same key. All requests go to `https://api.minima.sh`.
14	</Aside>
15	
16	## 1. Quickstart with curl
17	
18	Exercise the core endpoints with nothing but `curl` and `jq` — the fastest way to confirm your key works.
19	
20	```bash
21	# Service health (the only keyless endpoint)
22	curl -s https://api.minima.sh/v1/health | jq
23	
24	# A recommendation
25	REC=$(curl -s https://api.minima.sh/v1/recommend \
26	  -H "authorization: Bearer [REDACTED_BEARER_TOKEN]" \
27	  -H 'content-type: application/json' \
28	  -d '{"task":{"task":"Classify this support ticket by urgency.","task_type":"classification"},
29	       "cost_quality_tradeoff":2}')
30	echo "$REC" | jq '.recommended_model.model_id, .decision_basis, .recommendation_id'
31	
32	# Close the loop
33	curl -s https://api.minima.sh/v1/feedback \
34	  -H "authorization: Bearer [REDACTED_BEARER_TOKEN]" \
35	  -H 'content-type: application/json' \
36	  -d "{\"recommendation_id\":$(echo "$REC" | jq .recommendation_id),
37	       \"chosen_model_id\":\"claude-haiku-4-5\",\"outcome\":\"success\",\"quality_score\":0.92}" | jq
38	```
39	
40	## 2. The core loop
41	
42	The whole value loop with the Python SDK: recommend → (you run the model) → feedback. Report realized tokens and cost so the cost ranking sharpens.
43	
44	```python
45	from minima_client import MinimaClient
46	
47	with MinimaClient("https://api.minima.sh", api_key="mbt_…") as minima:
48	    rec = minima.recommend(
49	        "Summarize this incident report into 3 bullet points.",
50	        cost_quality_tradeoff=3,
51	        baseline_model_id="claude-opus-4-8",  # what you'd use without Minima -> honest savings
52	    )
53	    print(rec.recommended_model.model_id, rec.decision_basis)
54	
55	    # ... run rec.recommended_model.model_id in your own stack ...
56	
57	    minima.feedback(
58	        rec.recommendation_id,
59	        rec.recommended_model.model_id,
60	        "success",
61	        quality_score=0.95,
62	        input_tokens=1180,
63	        output_tokens=320,
64	        actual_cost_usd=0.0028,
65	        verified_in_production=True,
66	    )
67	```
68	
69	## 3. Constraints and the slider
70	
71	Hard `Constraints` (provider whitelist, quality floor, cost ceiling, deny-list) plus sweeping `cost_quality_tradeoff` from 0→10 to watch Minima walk the cost-vs-quality frontier for the same task.
72	
73	```python
74	from minima_client import MinimaClient, TaskInput, Constraints
75	
76	task = TaskInput(task="Refactor this 200-line module for readability.", task_type="code")
77	
78	with MinimaClient("https://api.minima.sh", api_key="mbt_…") as minima:
79	    rec = minima.recommend(
80	        task,
81	        constraints=Constraints(
82	            allowed_providers=["anthropic", "google"],
83	            min_quality=0.8,
84	            max_cost_per_call=0.02,
85	        ),
86	    )
87	    print("constrained pick:", rec.recommended_model.model_id)
88	
89	    for cq in (0, 5, 10):
90	        r = minima.recommend(task, cost_quality_tradeoff=cq)
91	        print(f"slider {cq:>2}: {r.recommended_model.model_id}  "
92	              f"~${r.recommended_model.est_cost_usd:.4f}")
93	```
94	
95	## 4. Multi-step workflow
96	
97	`POST /v1/recommend/workflow` routes each step of a pipeline independently — a cheap model for classify/extract, a stronger one for the hard reasoning step — and reports total cost versus the all-premium baseline. Each step gets its own `recommendation_id` for per-step feedback.
98	
99	```python
100	from minima_client import MinimaClient, WorkflowRequest, WorkflowStep, TaskInput
101	
102	req = WorkflowRequest(
103	    steps=[
104	        WorkflowStep(step_id="extract",
105	                     task=TaskInput(task="Extract entities from the email.",
106	                                    task_type="extraction")),
107	        WorkflowStep(step_id="reason",
108	                     task=TaskInput(task="Decide the next action given the entities.",
109	                                    task_type="reasoning", difficulty="hard")),
110	    ],
111	    cost_quality_tradeoff=4,
112	)
113	
114	with MinimaClient("https://api.minima.sh", api_key="mbt_…") as minima:
115	    wf = minima.recommend_workflow(req)
116	    for step in wf.steps:
117	        print(step.step_id, "→", step.recommendation.recommended_model.model_id)
118	    print(f"total ${wf.total_est_cost_usd:.4f} vs all-premium ${wf.total_est_cost_if_all_premium:.4f}")
119	```
120	
121	## 5. Zero-code intake
122	
123	`minima_client.autocapture` auto-captures your existing LLM calls into Minima's memory with no call-site changes — useful for backfilling history from traffic you already run. It needs a Mubit key for the underlying memory.
124	
125	```python
126	from minima_client import autocapture
127	
128	autocapture.enable(api_key="[REDACTED]", endpoint="https://api.mubit.ai",
129	                   namespace="team-payments", user_id="svc-router")
130	
131	# ... your normal OpenAI / Anthropic / LiteLLM / Gemini calls run here, auto-captured ...
132	
133	autocapture.feedback(good=True)   # learn does NOT fabricate a success signal — close it explicitly
134	autocapture.disable()
135	```
136	
137	## 6. Production routing wrapper
138	
139	The shape you'd ship: recommend a model, run it via the official **Anthropic SDK** (streaming, real token usage), then feed the realized cost/quality back.
140	
141	```python
142	import anthropic
143	from minima_client import AsyncMinimaClient
144	
145	async def routed_call(minima: AsyncMinimaClient, client: anthropic.AsyncAnthropic,
146	                      prompt: str, *, cost_quality_tradeoff: float = 4) -> str:
147	    rec = await minima.recommend(prompt, cost_quality_tradeoff=cost_quality_tradeoff)
148	    model = rec.recommended_model.model_id
149	
150	    async with client.messages.stream(
151	        model=model, max_tokens=1024,
152	        messages=[{"role": "user", "content": prompt}],
153	    ) as stream:
154	        msg = await stream.get_final_message()
155	
156	    text = "".join(b.text for b in msg.content if b.type == "text")
157	    cost = rec.recommended_model.est_cost_usd  # or compute from msg.usage + your price table
158	    await minima.feedback(
159	        rec.recommendation_id, model, "success",
160	        quality_score=0.95,
161	        input_tokens=msg.usage.input_tokens,
162	        output_tokens=msg.usage.output_tokens,
163	        actual_cost_usd=cost,
164	        verified_in_production=True,
165	    )
166	    return text
167	```
168	
169	## 7. Measure your savings
170	
171	The measurement loop: declare your default model on `recommend`, report realized cost on `feedback`, then read the ledger. Both baselines are reported side by side — `vs_premium` (generous) and `vs_declared` (your own default; the honest one).
172	
173	```python
174	from minima_client import MinimaClient
175	
176	with MinimaClient("https://api.minima.sh", api_key="mbt_…") as minima:
177	    rec = minima.recommend(
178	        "Classify this ticket by urgency.",
179	        baseline_model_id="claude-opus-4-8",   # the model you'd have used
180	    )
181	    # ... run rec.recommended_model.model_id in your stack ...
182	    minima.feedback(
183	        rec.recommendation_id, rec.recommended_model.model_id, "success",
184	        quality_score=0.95, actual_cost_usd=0.0009,    # realized, not estimated
185	        input_tokens=420, output_tokens=80,
186	    )
187	
188	    report = minima.savings(days=30, group_by="task_type")
189	    est = report.summary.estimated
190	    print(f"saved ${est.savings_vs_declared_usd:.4f} vs your default "
191	          f"over {est.n_declared} calls "
192	          f"(coverage {report.health['feedback_coverage']:.0%})")
193	
194	    cal = minima.calibration(days=30)
195	    print("global ECE:", cal.reports[0].ece)   # 0 = predictions match reality
196	```
197	
198	---
199

204	- The full client surface: [Python Client SDK](/client-sdk/).

[tool_result] toolu_01MCoFY5V2rVMvMQQBuMdG91
1	# minima-tui
2	
3	The TypeScript/Ink harness + TUI for **Minima** — a cost-aware model-routing coding
4	agent. This package is the runtime that lives in your terminal; it talks to the Python
5	FastAPI recommender service (`src/minima/`) over the `/v1/*` HTTP contract and never
6	proxies LLM calls itself.
7	
8	```
9	recommend  ->  run the model yourself  ->  judge quality  ->  feedback
10	```
11	
12	## What's here
13	
14	A faithful port of `src/minima_harness/` (Python) → TypeScript:
15	
16	- **`src/minima/`** — typed async client for the recommender service (every `/v1/*`
17	  endpoint) + the integration layer (`config`, `mapping`, `meter`, `judge`, `router`,
18	  `MinimaAgent` route→run→judge→feedback loop).
19	- **`src/ai/`** — the LLM layer: types, streaming, provider registry, and three real
20	  providers — `openai-compat` (raw `fetch` SSE), `anthropic` (`@anthropic-ai/sdk`),
21	  `google` (`@google/genai`) — plus a hermetic `faux` provider for tests.
22	- **`src/agent/`** — the agent core: PI event taxonomy, `agentLoop` (parallel tool
23	  execution, before/afterToolCall hooks, max_turns, steering), and the `Agent` class.
24	- **`src/tools/`** — `read`/`write`/`edit`/`bash`/`ls` + an `objectSchema` helper.
25	- **`src/tui/`** — the Ink app (conversation + status bar + model picker) and the
26	  credential store (OS keychain via `keytar`, else a 0600 file).
27	- **`src/session/`** — append-only JSONL session tree + `SessionManager`.
28	- **`src/cli/main.ts`** — the `minima` entry point (`--print`, `--mode json`, interactive
29	  TUI, `minima config`).
30	
31	## Develop
32	
33	```bash
34	cd packages/tui
35	bun install
36	bun test          # 75 hermetic tests
37	bun run check     # tsc --noEmit
38	bun run lint      # biome
39	bun run build     # -> dist/minima (a self-contained native binary)
40	./dist/minima --help
41	```
42	
43	Tests are fully hermetic: the client injects a mock `fetch`, the faux provider scripts
44	LLM replies, and the Minima integration runs the full loop against an in-process mock
45	service — no network, no keys.
46	
47	## Use
48	
49	```bash
50	# set credentials (keychain, else ~/.minima-harness/config.env at 0600)
51	minima config set MUBIT_API_KEY ...
52	minima config set OPENAI_API_KEY ...
53	
54	# one-shot
55	minima -p "explain this repo"
56	
57	# event stream for scripts
58	minima --mode json "refactor foo" | jq .
59	
60	# interactive TUI
61	minima
62	```
63	
64	Routing auth (`MUBIT_API_KEY`) + one provider key is enough to start. `--offline`
65	bypasses the recommender; `--model`/`--provider` pin a model and skip routing.
66	
67	## Status
68	
69	Phases 0–7 complete: scaffold → client → AI layer (3 providers) → agent core → tools →
70	Minima integration → Ink TUI + CLI → compiled native binary (`bun build --compile`).
71	Remaining for full parity with the Python TUI: the rest of the overlays (session picker,
72	diff-approval gating, themes, goals), mouse capture, and `keytar`-bundled keychain in the
73	compiled binary (it falls back to the file store today).
74

103	/** Env vars that supply the API key for `provider` (resolution order = first set wins). */

---
title: What is Minima
description: A recommendation engine for LLM model routing. Cuts token spend without losing quality.
---

import { CardGroup, Card, Note, Tip } from '@components'

# What is Minima

A recommendation engine, not a router. Route every LLM call to the cheapest model that will actually succeed — then watch the picks get sharper as you report outcomes.

Minima **only recommends**. It does not proxy your call, execute a model, rewrite prompts, cache, or compress. You ask "which model should run this?", it answers, and you run the model yourself in your own stack — so it adds **zero latency to the actual LLM request**.

## Two ways to use Minima

<CardGroup cols={2}>
  <Card title="Minima SDK & API" icon="code" href="/sdk/getting-started">
    A hosted API and a typed Python client. Call `recommend`, run the model in your own stack, then `feedback` the outcome. Embed cost-aware routing directly in your app.
  </Card>
  <Card title="Harness CLI" icon="terminal" href="/harness/overview">
    `minima` — a cost-aware, memory-isolated terminal coding agent that routes every prompt through Minima, runs the chosen model, and closes the feedback loop for you.
  </Card>
</CardGroup>

## How it works

<CardGroup cols={2}>
  <Card title="Recommend" icon="circle-nodes">
    `POST /v1/recommend` — Minima recalls similar past `task → model → outcome` records, aggregates empirical success rates, and returns the cheapest model expected to clear your quality bar. Zero added latency to your actual LLM call.
  </Card>
  <Card title="Run it yourself" icon="rocket">
    Minima hands back a `recommended_model` and a `recommendation_id`. You run the model in your own stack — Minima never proxies, executes, or rewrites.
  </Card>
  <Card title="Feed back" icon="receipt">
    `POST /v1/feedback` — report the outcome, quality score, and realized tokens. Minima writes the outcome to memory, reinforces the exact neighbors that drove the pick, and promotes durable lessons.
  </Card>
  <Card title="Improve automatically" icon="bolt">
    Every feedback call makes the next recommendation sharper. The cost basis climbs from flat estimates → observed median → rescaled per-request as history accumulates.
  </Card>
</CardGroup>

```
  POST /v1/recommend  ──▶  you run the model  ──▶  POST /v1/feedback
   (recall + rank)          (your stack)            (write outcome, reinforce)
        ▲                                                    │
        └────────────── picks get sharper ──────────────────┘
```

## Key properties

- **Recommend-only.** No proxy, no prompt rewriting, no caching. Sits beside your calls, adds zero latency to them.
- **Memory-driven.** Backed by [Mubit](https://mubit.ai) — semantic k-NN recall, per-entry reinforcement, and lesson promotion.
- **Accurate cost ranking.** Three cost-basis tiers (rescaled → observed → estimate) correctly rank reasoning-heavy models that look cheap on paper but spend output tokens thinking.
- **Works cold.** Prior-only fallback + benchmark seeding means useful picks from day one.
- **Provable.** `GET /v1/savings` reports estimated *and realized* savings against honest baselines, and `GET /v1/calibration` shows whether `predicted_success` matches your real outcomes.
- **Bring your own memory.** Authenticate with your own Mubit API key — Minima reads and writes your `task → model → outcome` history in your Mubit instance, with `namespace` / `user_id` sub-scoping inside it.

<Tip>
New to Minima? Start with the [SDK Getting Started](/sdk/getting-started) for the hosted API, or jump to the [Harness CLI Overview](/harness/overview) if you want the terminal agent.
</Tip>

---
title: Getting Started
description: From an API key to a working recommendation and a closed feedback loop in a few minutes.
---

import { Steps, Step, Note, Tip } from '@components'

<PageHeader />

# Getting Started

Minima is a hosted API. There is nothing to install or run — you call the service with an API key, get a model recommendation, run that model in **your own** stack, and report the outcome so the next pick gets sharper. This walks you from a key to a closed feedback loop.

## Prerequisites

- **A Mubit API key** (`mbt_…`) — your Mubit data-plane key. Minima passes it through on each request to read and write your `task → model → outcome` history in Mubit; there is no separate Minima key. [Request access](https://minima.sh) if you don't have one yet.
- Nothing else. Minima computes its own embeddings server-side — there's no model, database, or runtime for you to operate.

<Note>
**Base URL.** All requests go to `https://api.minima.sh`. The examples below read your Mubit key from a `MUBIT_API_KEY` environment variable — `export MUBIT_API_KEY=[REDACTED] before running them.
</Note>

<Steps>
  <Step title="Make your first recommendation">
    ```bash
    curl -s https://api.minima.sh/v1/recommend \
      -H "authorization: Bearer [REDACTED_BEARER_TOKEN]" \
      -H 'content-type: application/json' \
      -d '{
            "task": {"task": "Summarize this 2-page incident report into 3 bullet points.",
                     "task_type": "summarization"},
            "cost_quality_tradeoff": 3
          }' | jq
    ```

    You get back a `recommendation_id`, a `recommended_model`, a ranked candidate list, a `fallback_model`, and a `decision_basis` (`memory` | `prior` | `llm`).
  </Step>
  <Step title="Run the model yourself">
    Minima hands back the pick — it never proxies, executes, or rewrites. Run the recommended model in your own stack with your own provider credentials. Keep the `recommendation_id`; you'll quote it back in the next step.
  </Step>
  <Step title="Close the loop">
    Tell Minima how it went. This is what makes the next recommendation sharper — and it populates the realized cost/token history that powers accurate cost ranking.

    ```bash
    curl -s https://api.minima.sh/v1/feedback \
      -H "authorization: Bearer [REDACTED_BEARER_TOKEN]" \
      -H 'content-type: application/json' \
      -d '{
            "recommendation_id": "<from step 1>",
            "chosen_model_id": "claude-haiku-4-5",
            "outcome": "success",
            "quality_score": 0.95,
            "input_tokens": 1180,
            "output_tokens": 320,
            "actual_cost_usd": 0.0028,
            "verified_in_production": true
          }' | jq
    ```
  </Step>
</Steps>

<Tip>
**Cold start.** On day one your account has little history, so early picks lean on capability priors (`decision_basis: "prior"`, a `cold_start` warning) and the cheap-LLM reasoner fires more often. As your `/v1/feedback` outcomes accumulate, recommendations shift to `decision_basis: "memory"` and the cost ranking sharpens automatically — see [How it gets better over time](/sdk/concepts#how-it-gets-better-over-time).
</Tip>

## Next steps

- Use the typed [Python Client SDK](/sdk/client-sdk) instead of raw `curl`.
- Read [Concepts](/sdk/concepts) to understand the slider, the cost-basis tiers, and the escalation path.
- Browse the [Examples](/sdk/examples) for constraints, workflows, and a production routing wrapper.
- Prefer the terminal? The [Harness CLI](/harness/overview) wraps this whole loop in a coding agent.
