Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive view. Let me compile my findings into a detailed report:

## Comprehensive Investigation Report: Minima Harness CLI

Based on my thorough exploration of `/Users/eldaru/Mubit/Minima/minima/packages/tui/`, here are my complete findings for documentation planning:

---

### **1. WHAT IT IS**

**Product/Binary Name:** `minima`

**Package:** `minima-tui` (v0.1.0)

**What it does:** A TypeScript/Ink terminal UI and cost-aware model-routing coding agent that:
- Acts as a terminal-based AI coding assistant
- Routes prompts through the **Minima recommender service** (Python FastAPI backend) to intelligently select the best LLM model for each task
- Executes tool calls (read, write, edit, bash) with permission gating
- Maintains per-repo memory isolation via Mubit's memory backend
- Supports multiple LLM providers (OpenAI, Anthropic Claude, Google Gemini, DeepSeek, xAI, Groq, OpenRouter)
- Streams responses in real-time with costs & quality tracking

**Entry point / Command name:** `minima` (compiled binary from `/src/cli/main.ts`)

**Architecture highlight:** A faithful TypeScript port of the Python `minima_harness`, it talks to the Python FastAPI recommender service over HTTP (`/v1/*` endpoints) but **never proxies LLM calls itself** — those go directly to provider APIs.

**Key files:**
- `/Users/eldaru/Mubit/Minima/minima/packages/tui/README.md` — overview
- `/Users/eldaru/Mubit/Minima/minima/packages/tui/package.json` — binary entry point (`src/cli/main.ts`)

---

### **2. COMMANDS & FEATURES**

**Slash Commands** (interactive TUI mode):

From the `COMMANDS` array in `app.tsx` (line 73–97), the user-facing commands are:

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `/model` | Select or pin a model (or 'auto' to unpin) |
| `/clear` | Clear chat messages |
| `/auth` | Sign in to Mubit & provision this repo's project |
| `/config` | Show/set API keys (MUBIT, GEMINI, ANTHROPIC, etc.) |
| `/config set <KEY> <value>` | Store a credential |
| `/config get <KEY>` | Retrieve a credential |
| `/help` | Show available commands list |
| `/quit` / `/exit` | Exit the application |
| `/cost` | Show cost meter totals |
| `/reconnect` | Reconnect routing client |
| `/new` | Start a fresh session |
| `/name <label>` | Set the session display name |
| `/session` | Show session info |
| `/tree` | View the session tree |
| `/fork <entry-id>` | Fork from an entry ID |
| `/clone` | Clone the current session |
| `/resume [id]` | Resume a session (optionally by id) |
| `/judge [on\|off]` | Toggle LLM judging on/off |
| `/thoughts [on\|off]` | Toggle streaming model's thinking |
| `/mouse [on\|off]` | Toggle mouse capture (scroll vs select/copy) |
| `/perms` | Show current tool permission grants |
| `/undo` | Undo last AI change (git checkout) |
| `/compact` | Summarize old turns to free context |
| `/plan` | Toggle plan mode (read-only) |

**CLI Flags** (non-interactive modes):

From `main.ts` (lines 151–241):

```
minima [prompt] [--print|--mode json] [options]
minima auth              # sign in to Mubit + provision this repo's project
minima config [set|get]  # manage stored credentials

Options:
  -p, --print              # one-shot: print the reply and exit
  --mode {interactive|print|json}
  --model ID               # pin a model (bypasses routing)
  --provider NAME          # provider for a pinned --model
  --thinking LEVEL         # off|minimal|low|medium|high|xhigh
  --offline                # bypass Minima routing
  -t, --tools LIST         # comma-separated tool allowlist
  -xt, --exclude-tools LIST
  -nt, --no-tools
  -h, --help
```

**Run modes:**
- **Interactive TUI (default):** `minima` — full Ink UI with conversation, status bar, model picker
- **One-shot print:** `minima -p "explain this repo"` — run & print final answer, exit
- **Event stream:** `minima --mode json "refactor foo" | jq .` — stream AgentEvents as JSON lines for scripting

**Built-in Tools** (agents can invoke these with permission):

From `/src/tools/builtin.ts` (line 35–49):

1. **`read`** — Read file contents
2. **`write`** — Create new files
3. **`edit`** — Modify existing files
4. **`bash`** — Run shell commands (with timeout & abort support)
5. **`ls`** — List directory contents
6. **`glob`** — Glob pattern matching
7. **`grep`** — Text search with regex
8. **`todowrite`** — Special tool for plan/todo files
9. **`web_fetch`** — Fetch & parse HTTP content

Users can exclude tools via CLI: `-xt bash,write` or pin specific ones via `-t read,ls`.

**Terminal UI Features:**

From `app.tsx`:
- **Conversation view** with scrolling (Page Up/Down, Home/End, Ctrl+G/Ctrl+E)
- **Status bar** showing: model selection (pinned vs. Minima routing), cost totals, context %, token counts
- **Model picker** (Ctrl+L) — interactive selector for 8 default models + custom models
- **Command palette** (Ctrl+P) — slash-command autocomplete
- **Config overlay** (Ctrl+P → /config) — interactive editor for API keys
- **Permission overlay** — interactive grant/deny for sensitive tools (read-only auto-approves by directory)
- **Session picker** (/resume) — browse and resume previous session trees
- **Streaming display** — shows assistant reply + tool calls in real-time
- **Thinking display** (when enabled) — shows model's reasoning for models with extended thinking

**Keyboard Shortcuts** (from `app.tsx` lines 673–724):

| Key | Action |
|-----|--------|
| Ctrl+C (once) | Prepare to quit (Ctrl+C again to exit) |
| Ctrl+C (while running) | Abort the in-flight run |
| Esc | Abort the in-flight run |
| Ctrl+L | Open model picker |
| Ctrl+P | Open command palette |
| Ctrl+R | Toggle route mode (auto vs. confirm) |
| Ctrl+G | Jump to top of chat |
| Ctrl+E | Jump to bottom of chat |
| Page Up / Page Down | Scroll up/down |
| Shift+Space | Scroll down (alternate) |
| ↑/↓ in command/session picker | Navigate |
| ⏎ (Return) | Execute command/pick session |
| Tab | Auto-complete slash command |
| ↑/↓ at prompt | Cycle through input history |

**Permission System:**

From `permissions.ts` and `app.tsx`:
- **Read tools** (`read`, `ls`, `glob`, `grep`) — auto-approved within `cwd` and allowed parent directories; can grant "always" for a directory
- **Write tools** (`write`, `edit`) — always prompt; can grant "always" for the tool
- **Dangerous tools** (`bash`) — always prompt; can grant "always" for the tool
- **Plan mode** (`/plan`) — read-only toggle that blocks `write`, `edit`, `bash` completely

---

### **3. CONFIGURATION**

**Config file locations & format:**

From `config_store.ts`:

1. **Per-user secrets** (keychain-first):
   - **Primary:** OS keychain via `keytar` native module (macOS Keychain, Linux Secret Service, Windows Credential Manager)
   - **Fallback:** `~/.minima-harness/config.env` (0600 mode, plaintext)
   - **Format:** `KEY=VALUE` lines

2. **Project .env file** (project-scoped):
   - `.env.harness` or `.env` in the current directory
   - **Precedence:** Project .env > shell env > user store

3. **Per-repo project metadata:**
   - `~/.minima-harness/projects.json` — maps repo identity → Mubit instance/project/namespace

**Environment Variables:**

From `config.ts` and `config_store.ts`:

| Variable | Purpose | Example | Required? |
|----------|---------|---------|-----------|
| `MUBIT_API_KEY` | Routing auth & memory backend access | (secret key) | **Yes** |
| `MINIMA_URL` | Minima recommender endpoint | `https://api.minima.sh` (default) | No |
| `MINIMA_API_KEY` | Minima auth (optional; falls back to MUBIT_API_KEY) | (secret key) | No |
| `MUBIT_ENDPOINT` | Mubit memory backend URL | (overrides default) | No |
| `MUBIT_CONSOLE_URL` | Mubit console URL (used by `/auth`) | `https://console.mubit.ai` (default) | No |
| `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` | Claude API key | (secret key) | Conditional* |
| `OPENAI_API_KEY` | OpenAI API key | (secret key) | Conditional* |
| `GEMINI_API_KEY` | Google Gemini API key | (secret key) | Conditional* |
| `DEEPSEEK_API_KEY` | DeepSeek API key | (secret key) | Conditional* |
| `XAI_API_KEY` | xAI/Grok API key | (secret key) | Conditional* |
| `GROQ_API_KEY` | Groq API key | (secret key) | Conditional* |
| `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` | OpenRouter aggregator API key | (secret key) | Conditional* |
| `MINIMA_NAMESPACE` | Per-repo memory lane (project isolation) | (custom string) | No |
| `MINIMA_TIMEOUT` | Request timeout in seconds | `30` (default) | No |

*At least one provider key is needed to run the LLM.

**Supported Providers** (from `provider_catalog.ts`):

- **Anthropic** (Claude) — `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`, `ANTHROPIC_OAUTH_TOKEN`
- **OpenAI** — `OPENAI_API_KEY`
- **Google Gemini** — `GEMINI_API_KEY`, `GOOGLE_API_KEY`, `GOOGLE_GENAI_API_KEY`
- **DeepSeek** — `DEEPSEEK_API_KEY` (cheap, open-weight)
- **xAI (Grok)** — `XAI_API_KEY`
- **Groq** — `GROQ_API_KEY` (fast inference)
- **OpenRouter** — `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` (aggregator)

**Default Model Catalog** (from `main.ts` lines 64–143):

8 seed models (users can add more via `/model` picker):
- `gpt-4o-mini`, `gpt-4o` (OpenAI)
- `claude-haiku-4-5`, `claude-sonnet-4-6`, `claude-opus-4-8` (Anthropic)
- `gemini-2.5-flash`, `gemini-2.5-pro` (Google)
- `deepseek-chat` (DeepSeek)

**Config Sections** (from `config_store.ts`):

1. **LLM provider keys** — all provider API keys
2. **Mubit / Minima routing** — `MUBIT_API_KEY`, `MINIMA_URL`, auth keys

---

### **4. INSTALLATION / SETUP**

**Building from source:**

```bash
cd packages/tui
bun install
bun run check     # type-check
bun run lint      # biome check
bun test          # 75 hermetic tests
bun run build     # produces dist/minima (single native binary)
./dist/minima --help
```

**Prerequisites:**
- **Bun >= 1.2** (from `package.json` engines)
- **One provider API key** (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, DeepSeek, Groq, xAI, or OpenRouter)
- **MUBIT_API_KEY** (Mubit routing + memory backend access)

**First-time setup:**

1. **Set credentials:**
   ```bash
   minima config set MUBIT_API_KEY <your-mubit-key>
   minima config set OPENAI_API_KEY <your-openai-key>
   # or ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, GEMINI_API_KEY, etc.
   ```

2. **Or use the browser login flow (recommended):**
   ```bash
   minima auth
   # Opens browser → signs in to Mubit → provisions this repo's project → stores MUBIT_API_KEY
   ```

3. **Start the TUI:**
   ```bash
   minima
   ```

**Auth flow** (`/auth` command or `minima auth` CLI):
- Launches browser to Mubit console
- User authorizes
- Returns `MUBIT_API_KEY` + instance/project metadata
- Stores key securely (keychain or `~/.minima-harness/config.env`)
- Persists project mapping in `~/.minima-harness/projects.json` for per-repo isolation

**Config CLI** (`minima config`):
- `minima config` — list all config keys with masked secrets
- `minima config set <KEY> <value>` — store a credential
- `minima config get <KEY>` — retrieve a credential

**Key integration flows:**

1. **Start (interactive):** `main() → loadEnvFiles() → seedDefaultModels() → new MinimaAgent() → render(HarnessApp) → event loop`

2. **Prompt → Response:**
   - User types → `onSubmit()` → `agent.promptRouted(text)` 
   - → Route via Minima (`/v1/route`)
   - → Run agent loop (stream LLM, execute tools, retry)
   - → Judge quality
   - → POST feedback to `/v1/feedback`

3. **Permission gating:** `beforeToolCall` hook checks permissions, renders overlay if needed, resolves promise.

4. **Session persistence:** Every turn appended to `~/.minima-harness/sessions/<sessionId>.jsonl`

10	# Installation
11	
12	The harness ships as a self-contained native binary built from the `packages/tui` workspace with [Bun](https://bun.sh). Build it once, then authenticate and start.
13	
14	## Prerequisites
15	
16	- **[Bun](https://bun.sh) ≥ 1.2** — the build toolchain and test runner.
17	- **A Mubit API key** (`MUBIT_API_KEY`) — routing + memory-backend auth. `minima auth` can obtain one for you (see below).
18	- **At least one LLM provider key** — the harness runs the model itself, so you need a key for whichever provider(s) you want to call: OpenAI, Anthropic, Google Gemini, DeepSeek, xAI, Groq, or OpenRouter. Local runtimes (Ollama, vLLM, LM Studio) need no key.
19	
20	## Build from source
21	
22	<Steps>
23	  <Step title="Install dependencies">
24	    ```bash
25	    cd packages/tui
26	    bun install
27	    ```
28	  </Step>
29	  <Step title="(Optional) run the checks">
30	    ```bash
31	    bun test          # hermetic test suite (no network, no keys)
32	    bun run check     # tsc --noEmit
33	    bun run lint      # biome
34	    ```
35	  </Step>
36	  <Step title="Compile the binary">
37	    ```bash
38	    bun run build     # -> dist/minima (a self-contained native binary)
39	    ./dist/minima --help
40	    ```
41	
42	    Put `dist/minima` on your `PATH` (or symlink it) so you can invoke it as `minima` from any repo.
43	  </Step>
44	</Steps>
45	
46	## First-time setup
47	
48	Pick one of the two paths below.
49	
50	### Recommended — browser login
51	
52	`minima auth` opens your browser, signs you in to Mubit, provisions a Mubit **project scoped to the current repo**, stores your `MUBIT_API_KEY`, and records the project mapping for per-repo memory isolation.
53	
54	```bash
55	cd your-repo
56	minima auth
57	# Opens the browser to authorize; on success stores MUBIT_API_KEY (+ MINIMA_URL)
58	# and writes the repo → project mapping to ~/.minima-harness/projects.json
59	```
60	
61	<Note>
62	`minima auth` accepts `--region eu|us` to choose a Mubit region. The console URL defaults to `https://console.mubit.ai` and can be overridden with `MUBIT_CONSOLE_URL`.
63	</Note>
64	
65	### Manual — set credentials directly
66	
67	If you already have keys, store them with `minima config`:
68	
69	```bash
70	minima config set MUBIT_API_KEY   mbt_…
71	minima config set OPENAI_API_KEY  sk-…
72	# or ANTHROPIC_API_KEY / GEMINI_API_KEY / DEEPSEEK_API_KEY / …
73	```
74	
75	Secrets go to your OS keychain when available, otherwise to `~/.minima-harness/config.env` (mode `0600`). See [Configuration](/harness/configuration) for the full precedence rules and every key.
76	
77	## Start
78	
79	```bash
80	minima            # interactive TUI (default)
81	minima -p "explain this repo"          # one-shot answer, then exit
82	minima --mode json "refactor foo" | jq # machine-readable event stream
83	```
84	
85	<Tip>
86	`MUBIT_API_KEY` (routing) plus **one** provider key is enough to start. From there, `--offline` bypasses the recommender and `--model` / `--provider` pin a specific model. See [CLI usage](/harness/cli).
87	</Tip>
88
Now I'll rewrite the page to lead with Homebrew and keep build-from-source as an optional developer path.

import { PageHeader, Steps, Step, Note, Tip, Accordion } from '@components'

<PageHeader />

# Installation

The `minima` harness is distributed as a self-contained native binary through the [`mubit-ai/homebrew-minima`](https://github.com/mubit-ai/homebrew-minima) Homebrew tap. Install it, authenticate, and start.

## Prerequisites

- **[Homebrew](https://brew.sh)** (macOS or Linux).
- **A Mubit API key** (`MUBIT_API_KEY`) — routing + memory-backend auth. `minima auth` can obtain one for you (see below).
- **At least one LLM provider key** — the harness runs the model itself, so you need a key for whichever provider(s) you want to call: OpenAI, Anthropic, Google Gemini, DeepSeek, xAI, Groq, or OpenRouter. Local runtimes (Ollama, vLLM, LM Studio) need no key.

## Install with Homebrew

```bash
brew install mubit-ai/minima/minima
```

That taps [`mubit-ai/homebrew-minima`](https://github.com/mubit-ai/homebrew-minima) and installs the `minima` formula in one step. Prefer to tap explicitly? It's equivalent to:

```bash
brew tap mubit-ai/minima
brew install minima
```

Verify it's on your `PATH`, and upgrade later with Homebrew:

```bash
minima --help
brew upgrade minima      # pull the latest release
```

<Note>
The Homebrew formula ships the compiled binary, which bundles no OS keychain module — so credentials fall back to a `~/.minima-harness/config.env` file (mode `0600`). See [Configuration](/harness/configuration#managing-credentials).
</Note>

<Accordion title="Build from source (for development)">
Contributing to the harness? Build the binary yourself from the `packages/tui` workspace with [Bun](https://bun.sh) ≥ 1.2:

```bash
cd packages/tui
bun install
bun test          # hermetic test suite (no network, no keys)
bun run check     # tsc --noEmit
bun run lint      # biome
bun run build     # -> dist/minima (a self-contained native binary)
./dist/minima --help
```

Put `dist/minima` on your `PATH` (or symlink it) to invoke it as `minima` from any repo.
</Accordion>

## First-time setup"}

# Minima Docs: nav cleanup, `/`-command notes, and "Minima CLI" rename

## Context

Follow-up refinements to the now-live Vocs docs (`docs-site/`, branch `docs/minima-full-docs-update`). The user wants four changes:

1. Drop the standalone **"Get API Key"** CTA from the top nav and instead surface that link where a new user actually needs it — the Installation **Prerequisites**.
2. Make clear that `minima auth` has an in-app equivalent: the **`/auth`** slash command.
3. On the Configuration page, make clear that credential management isn't CLI-only — the **`/config`** slash commands (`/config`, `/config set <KEY> <value>`, `/config get <KEY>`) do the same inside the interactive TUI.
4. Rename the product label **"Harness CLI" → "Minima CLI"** across the docs.

All targets are already known from a grep (below); no new exploration needed.

## Changes

### 1. Remove "Get API Key" from the top nav
`docs-site/vocs.config.ts` — delete the `topNav` entry:
```ts
{ text: 'Get API Key', link: 'https://console.mubit.ai' }
```
Result: top nav becomes **SDK · Minima CLI · API Reference** (no external CTA).

`public/styles/global.css` styled that link via `.vocs_ExternalLink`. With no external nav links left it becomes **inert (harmless) dead CSS** — leave the file and its `head()` injection in place (still carries the gated PostHog block, and lets a future external nav link be styled). Note it; do not delete.

### 2. Move the "Get API Key" link into Installation → Prerequisites
`docs-site/pages/harness/installation.mdx` — in the Prerequisites bullet for the Mubit API key, add the console link and both ways to obtain the key:
> A Mubit API key (`MUBIT_API_KEY`) … **Get one from the [Mubit console](https://console.mubit.ai)**, or run `minima auth` (see below) to have it created and stored for you.

### 3. Note the `/auth` in-app equivalent
`docs-site/pages/harness/installation.mdx` — in the "Recommended — browser login" subsection, add that the same flow runs inside the TUI via the **`/auth`** slash command (cross-link to [Interactive TUI](/harness/interactive)). (`/auth` already appears in the interactive command table — this just points to it from install.)

### 4. Document `/config` slash commands on the Configuration page
`docs-site/pages/harness/configuration.mdx` — in "Managing credentials", after the `minima config …` CLI block, add a short note + code block showing the interactive equivalent:
```
/config                     # open the config editor / list keys
/config set <KEY> <value>   # store a credential
/config get <KEY>           # print a stored value
```
Cross-link to [Interactive TUI](/harness/interactive).

### 5. Rename "Harness CLI" → "Minima CLI"
Replace the exact label **"Harness CLI"** with **"Minima CLI"** at every occurrence (keep the `/harness/*` **routes** and standalone "harness" / "minima harness" prose unchanged — only the product label changes):

- `docs-site/vocs.config.ts:67` — topNav tab text.
- `docs-site/components/sidebar.ts` — the `/harness` group `text` (line 47), the home-sidebar item "Harness CLI — Overview" (line 24), and the comment on line 15.
- `docs-site/pages/harness/overview.mdx` — frontmatter `title:` (line 2) and the `# Harness CLI` H1 (line 10).
- `docs-site/pages/index.mdx` — Card `title="Harness CLI"` (line 20) and the "Harness CLI Overview" link text (line 59).
- `docs-site/pages/sdk/getting-started.mdx:71` — the "[Harness CLI]" link text.

## Notes / decisions
- **Routes stay `/harness/*`.** The request is about the display label, not URLs; renaming the directory would churn every internal link, the sidebar keys, and the nav `match`. Left as-is.
- `PageHeader` derives its eyebrow from the sidebar group text via `getSectionForPath` — renaming the `/harness` group to "Minima CLI" updates that eyebrow automatically; no code change needed.

## Verification
From `docs-site/`:
1. `npm run typecheck` — clean.
2. `npm run build` — prerenders all 15 pages with no errors.
3. Grep the built output:
   - `grep -ri "get api key" dist/index.html` → **no match** (CTA gone from nav).
   - `grep -o "Minima CLI" dist/harness/overview/index.html` and the home page → present; `grep -r "Harness CLI" dist` → **no matches**.
   - Confirm `https://console.mubit.ai` appears in `dist/harness/installation/index.html`, and `/config` / `/auth` mentions render on the installation + configuration pages.
4. `npm run preview` → spot-check the nav (3 tabs) and the two edited pages return 200.

# Minima Docs: nav cleanup, `/`-command notes, and "Minima CLI" rename

## Context

Follow-up refinements to the now-live Vocs docs (`docs-site/`, branch `docs/minima-full-docs-update`). The user wants four changes:

1. Drop the standalone **"Get API Key"** CTA from the top nav and instead surface that link where a new user actually needs it — the Installation **Prerequisites**.
2. Make clear that `minima auth` has an in-app equivalent: the **`/auth`** slash command.
3. On the Configuration page, make clear that credential management isn't CLI-only — the **`/config`** slash commands (`/config`, `/config set <KEY> <value>`, `/config get <KEY>`) do the same inside the interactive TUI.
4. Rename the product label **"Harness CLI" → "Minima CLI"** across the docs.

All targets are already known from a grep (below); no new exploration needed.

## Changes

### 1. Remove "Get API Key" from the top nav
`docs-site/vocs.config.ts` — delete the `topNav` entry:
```ts
{ text: 'Get API Key', link: 'https://console.mubit.ai' }
```
Result: top nav becomes **SDK · Minima CLI · API Reference** (no external CTA).

`public/styles/global.css` styled that link via `.vocs_ExternalLink`. With no external nav links left it becomes **inert (harmless) dead CSS** — leave the file and its `head()` injection in place (still carries the gated PostHog block, and lets a future external nav link be styled). Note it; do not delete.

### 2. Move the "Get API Key" link into Installation → Prerequisites
`docs-site/pages/harness/installation.mdx` — in the Prerequisites bullet for the Mubit API key, add the console link and both ways to obtain the key:
> A Mubit API key (`MUBIT_API_KEY`) … **Get one from the [Mubit console](https://console.mubit.ai)**, or run `minima auth` (see below) to have it created and stored for you.

### 3. Note the `/auth` in-app equivalent
`docs-site/pages/harness/installation.mdx` — in the "Recommended — browser login" subsection, add that the same flow runs inside the TUI via the **`/auth`** slash command (cross-link to [Interactive TUI](/harness/interactive)). (`/auth` already appears in the interactive command table — this just points to it from install.)

### 4. Document `/config` slash commands on the Configuration page
`docs-site/pages/harness/configuration.mdx` — in "Managing credentials", after the `minima config …` CLI block, add a short note + code block showing the interactive equivalent:
```
/config                     # open the config editor / list keys
/config set <KEY> <value>   # store a credential
/config get <KEY>           # print a stored value
```
Cross-link to [Interactive TUI](/harness/interactive).

### 5. Rename "Harness CLI" → "Minima CLI"
Replace the exact label **"Harness CLI"** with **"Minima CLI"** at every occurrence (keep the `/harness/*` **routes** and standalone "harness" / "minima harness" prose unchanged — only the product label changes):

- `docs-site/vocs.config.ts:67` — topNav tab text.
- `docs-site/components/sidebar.ts` — the `/harness` group `text` (line 47), the home-sidebar item "Harness CLI — Overview" (line 24), and the comment on line 15.
- `docs-site/pages/harness/overview.mdx` — frontmatter `title:` (line 2) and the `# Harness CLI` H1 (line 10).
- `docs-site/pages/index.mdx` — Card `title="Harness CLI"` (line 20) and the "Harness CLI Overview" link text (line 59).
- `docs-site/pages/sdk/getting-started.mdx:71` — the "[Harness CLI]" link text.

## Notes / decisions
- **Routes stay `/harness/*`.** The request is about the display label, not URLs; renaming the directory would churn every internal link, the sidebar keys, and the nav `match`. Left as-is.
- `PageHeader` derives its eyebrow from the sidebar group text via `getSectionForPath` — renaming the `/harness` group to "Minima CLI" updates that eyebrow automatically; no code change needed.

item.text:
Right — "by" should be centered in the gap, i.e., equal distance on both sides. The trouble is the by→Mubit gap depends on the *rendered* width of "minima", which varies by system font, so my estimates keep drifting.

The robust fix: pin "minima" and "by" to fixed widths with SVG `textLength`, so their right edges are deterministic — then I can place everything with exact, equal 16px gaps regardless of the viewer's font.

tool:Bash
{"command":"\
SRC_DARK=/Users/eldaru/Mubit/Documentation/docs/public/images/logo-dark.svg && \
SRC_LIGHT=/Users/eldaru/Mubit/Documentation/docs/public/images/logo-light.svg && \
INNER_DARK=$(sed -n '2,4p' "$SRC_DARK") && \
INNER_LIGHT=$(sed -n '2,4p' "$SRC_LIGHT") && \
cat > public/images/logo-dark.svg <<'HEAD'
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 198 32" fill="none" role="img" aria-label="minima by Mubit">
  <!-- Deterministic layout: minima (textLength 84) and by (textLength 14) have fixed widths,
       so the two gaps are exactly equal (16px) and "by" is centered between minima and the Mubit mark. -->
  <text x="0" y="23" font-family="'Manrope', ui-sans-serif, system-ui, -apple-system, 'Segoe UI', Roboto, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif" font-size="26" font-weight="800" textLength="84" lengthAdjust="spacing" fill="#f7f6f3">minima</text>
  <text x="100" y="23" font-family="'Manrope', ui-sans-serif, system-ui, -apple-system, 'Segoe UI', Roboto, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif" font-size="13" font-weight="500" textLength="14" lengthAdjust="spacing" fill="#8b8b90">by</text>
  <svg x="130" y="9" width="64" height="14" viewBox="109 141 1662 362" preserveAspectRatio="xMidYMid meet">
HEAD
printf '%s\n' "$INNER_DARK" >> public/images/logo-dark.svg && \
printf '  </svg>\n</svg>\n' >> public/images/logo-dark.svg && \
cat > public/images/logo-light.svg <<'HEAD'
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 198 32" fill="none" role="img" aria-label="minima by Mubit">
  <!-- Deterministic layout: minima (textLength 84) and by (textLength 14) have fixed widths,
       so the two gaps are exactly equal (16px) and "by" is centered between minima and the Mubit mark. -->
  <text x="0" y="23" font-family="'Manrope', ui-sans-serif, system-ui, -apple-system, 'Segoe UI', Roboto, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif" font-size="26" font-weight="800" textLength="84" lengthAdjust="spacing" fill="#18181b">minima</text>
  <text x="100" y="23" font-family="'Manrope', ui-sans-serif, system-ui, -apple-system, 'Segoe UI', Roboto, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif" font-size="13" font-weight="500" textLength="14" lengthAdjust="spacing" fill="#6b6b70">by</text>
  <svg x="130" y="9" width="64" height="14" viewBox="109 141 1662 362" preserveAspectRatio="xMidYMid meet">
HEAD
printf '%s\n' "$INNER_LIGHT" >> public/images/logo-light.svg && \
printf '  </svg>\n</svg>\n' >> public/images/logo-light.svg && \
npm run build 2>&1 | tail -3 && \
echo "=== dark logo layout ===" && grep -oE 'x="0"|textLength="84"|x="100"|textLength="14"|<svg x="130"' dist/images/logo-dark.svg","description":"Rebuild logos with textLength-pinned widths for exact equal gaps","timeout":180000}

Now the layout is deterministic and font-independent:
- **minima** pinned to 84px wide (ends at x=84), **by** pinned to 14px (x=100–114), Mubit mark at x=130.
- That's an exact **16px gap on each side of "by"** — so "by" is mathematically centered between minima and the Mubit icon, and it stays centered regardless of which system font renders it (the previous drift is gone because `textLength` fixes the text widths).

Rebuilt and verified the geometry. Preview: `cd docs-site && npm run dev`.

If "minima" looks a touch tight/loose from the width-pinning, I can adjust the pinned width (and shift the rest) — but the centering itself is now locked. Want me to commit and push to `docs/minima-full-docs-update`?

item.text: Got it — the `textLength="84"` pin tightened "minima" below its natural width, which is what's making it look packed. I'll loosen it (larger `textLength` = more inter-letter spacing) and shift "by" and the Mubit mark right to keep the equal 16px gaps.
